1	# District Materials Commerce & Logistics Portal
2	
3	A web-based portal for managing district-wide distribution of educational materials. It provides role-aware workflows for students (browsing, ordering, favorites), instructors (course plans, approvals), clerks (distribution, ledger), moderators (comment queue), and administrators (users, analytics, settings). Built with Go, Fiber, SQLite, HTMX, and Alpine.js.
4	
5	## Prerequisites
6	
7	- Go 1.22 or later
8	- GCC (required to compile the `mattn/go-sqlite3` CGo driver)
9	  - macOS: `xcode-select --install`
10	  - Ubuntu/Debian: `sudo apt install gcc`
11	  - Windows: install [TDM-GCC](https://jmeubank.github.io/tdm-gcc/) or use WSL
12	
13	## Setup
14	
15	1. **Clone the repository**
16	
17	   ```bash
18	   git clone <repo-url>
19	   cd w2t86
20	   ```
21	
22	2. **Install Go dependencies**
23	
24	   ```bash
25	   go mod tidy
26	   ```
27	
28	3. **Download HTMX and Alpine.js**
29	
30	   Place the minified files in `web/static/js/`:
31	
32	   ```bash
33	   # HTMX 2.x
34	   curl -Lo web/static/js/htmx.min.js \
35	     https://unpkg.com/htmx.org@2/dist/htmx.min.js
36	
37	   # Alpine.js 3.x
38	   curl -Lo web/static/js/alpine.min.js \
39	     https://unpkg.com/alpinejs@3/dist/cdn.min.js
40	   ```
41	
42	4. **Configure environment**
43	
44	   ```bash
45	   cp .env.example .env
46	   # Edit .env and fill in ENCRYPTION_KEY and SESSION_SECRET
47	   ```
48	
49	   Generate a random encryption key (32 bytes = 64 hex chars):
50	
51	   ```bash
52	   openssl rand -hex 32
53	   ```
54	
55	5. **Run the server**
56	
57	   ```bash
58	   go run ./cmd/server
59	   ```
60	
61	   The server listens on `http://localhost:3000` by default.
62	
63	## Docker
64	
65	### Quick start
66	```bash
67	cp .env.example .env
68	# Edit .env: set ENCRYPTION_KEY and SESSION_SECRET
69	docker compose up -d
70	```
71	
72	### Development (with live template reload)
73	```bash
74	docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.dev.yml up
75	```
76	
77	### Useful commands
78	```bash
79	make docker-logs     # tail logs
80	make docker-down     # stop
81	make test            # run tests locally
82	```
83	
84	## Environment Variables
85	
86	```
87	PORT=3000
88	DB_PATH=data/portal.db
89	ENCRYPTION_KEY=replace_with_64_hex_chars_32_bytes
90	SESSION_SECRET=replace_with_random_secret
91	APP_ENV=development
92	```
93	
94	| Variable         | Description                                          | Default           |
95	|------------------|------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|
96	| `PORT`           | HTTP listen port                                     | `3000`            |
97	| `DB_PATH`        | Path to the SQLite database file                     | `data/portal.db`  |
98	| `ENCRYPTION_KEY` | 64-char hex string (32 bytes) for AES-256-GCM fields | *(required)*      |
99	| `SESSION_SECRET` | Random secret used to sign session tokens            | *(required)*      |
100	| `APP_ENV`        | `development` (hot-reload templates) or `production` | `development`     |
101	
102	## Default Credentials
103	
104	| Username | Password       | Role  |
105	|----------|----------------|-------|
106	| `admin`  | `ChangeMe123!` | admin |
107	
108	**Change the admin password immediately after first login.**
109	
110	The default admin account is inserted by the initial migration. Update it via the Admin Settings page or directly in the database.
111	
112	## Available Roles
113	
114	| Role         | Capabilities                                                        |
115	|--------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
116	| `student`    | Browse materials, place orders, manage favorites, inbox             |
117	| `instructor` | Course plans, approve orders, inbox                                 |
118	| `clerk`      | Distribution events, ledger, backorder management, inbox            |
119	| `moderator`  | Review and act on reported comments, inbox                          |
120	| `admin`      | Full access: user management, analytics, all settings, all of above |
121	
122	## Project Structure
123	
124	```
125	w2t86/
126	├── cmd/
127	│   └── server/
128	│       └── main.go              # Entry point: wires and starts the server
129	├── internal/
130	│   ├── config/
131	│   │   └── config.go            # Environment-based configuration
132	│   ├── crypto/
133	│   │   └── crypto.go            # Password hashing + AES-256-GCM helpers
134	│   ├── db/
135	│   │   └── db.go                # SQLite open + migration runner
136	│   ├── handlers/
137	│   │   └── auth.go              # Login / logout HTTP handlers
138	│   ├── middleware/
139	│   │   ├── auth.go              # Session validation, GetUser helper
140	│   │   ├── ratelimit.go         # Sliding-window rate limiter
141	│   │   └── rbac.go              # Role-based access control
142	│   ├── models/
143	│   │   └── models.go            # Go structs for every DB table
144	│   ├── repository/
145	│   │   ├── sessions.go          # Session CRUD
146	│   │   └── users.go             # User CRUD
147	│   ├── scheduler/
148	│   │   └── scheduler.go         # Cron jobs: auto-close stale orders
149	│   └── services/
150	│       └── auth.go              # Login, logout, register business logic
151	├── migrations/
152	│   └── 001_schema.sql           # Full database schema
153	├── web/
154	│   ├── static/
155	│   │   ├── css/
156	│   │   │   └── app.css          # Hand-crafted application styles
157	│   │   └── js/
158	│   │       ├── app.js           # Vanilla JS utilities (toast, confirm, etc.)
159	│   │       ├── alpine.min.js    # Alpine.js 3.x (download separately)
160	│   │       └── htmx.min.js      # HTMX 2.x (download separately)
161	│   └── templates/
162	│       ├── layouts/
163	│       │   ├── base.html        # Full layout: sidebar + topbar (authenticated)
164	│       │   └── main.html        # Minimal layout: HTML shell (login page)
165	│       ├── pages/               # (reserved for future page templates)
166	│       ├── partials/
167	│       │   ├── login_form.html  # Login form partial (HTMX swap target)
168	│       │   └── toast.html       # Toast notification partial (hx-swap-oob)
169	│       ├── login.html           # Login page content
170	│       └── dashboard.html       # Dashboard page content
171	├── .env.example                 # Example environment configuration
172	├── go.mod
173	├── go.sum
174	└── README.md
175	```
176

Goal: Resolve all "Fail" and "Partial Pass" findings from the provided Static Audit Report.
Context: This is an on-prem district materials logistics portal. We need to prioritize security blockers and core business logic gaps.
1. Critical Security & Routing (The "Blockers")

    Secrets: Remove .env from the repository. Implement a check in internal/config to ensure required environment variables (especially encryption keys) are present, or the app must fail at startup. Rotate all leaked secrets.

    Broken Routes: Fix the HTMX mismatch in repo/web/templates/orders/detail.html. The "Cancel" action targets /admin/orders/{id}/cancel. Ensure this route is registered in main.go and implemented in internal/handlers/orders.go.

2. Core Business Logic (Missing Domain Models)

    Course Planning: Implement the missing "Course/Class Demand Planning" domain. Add tables for courses, course_sections, and course_plans to 001_schema.sql. Build the corresponding repository, service, and UI for instructors to manage these plans.

    Partial Fulfillment: Update the distribution flow in internal/services/distribution.go. When an order cannot be fully filled, split it: track issued_qty vs. requested_qty and correctly integrate the existing (but currently unused) backorder APIs.

    Exchange Rules: In internal/services/orders.go, update the exchange approval workflow. It must validate inventory availability for the replacement item before allowing the status transition to "approved."

3. Authorization & Integrity

    KPI Security: Secure the /api/stats/:stat endpoint. Add role-level authorization checks so that sensitive metrics (like GMV or active users) are only accessible to the "admin" role.

    Encryption Safety: Refactor the custom field storage in internal/services/admin.go. If encrypt=true is requested but the encryption key is invalid or missing, the request must fail with an error rather than silently falling back to plaintext.

4. Missing UI & UX Flows

    Browse History: Implement a "Resume Browsing" page. Create the route and template to display the history already being recorded in internal/repository/engagement.go.

5. Strengthening the Test Suite

    Assertions: Refactor internal/integration tests. Remove all instances where 500 Internal Server Error is an acceptable response. Ensure tests assert specific 2xx or 3xx statuses and check for expected content in the HTML body.

    New Tests: Add unit tests specifically for the new partial fulfillment logic and the exchange inventory check.

Verification Rule: After each major fix, run the relevant tests and verify that no regressions were introduced.

3	import "os"
4	
5	// Config holds all runtime configuration for the portal.
6	type Config struct {
7		Port          string // HTTP listen port, default "3000"
8		DBPath        string // SQLite file path, default "data/portal.db"
9		EncryptionKey string // 32-byte hex key for AES-256-GCM field encryption
10		SessionSecret string // secret used to sign/verify session tokens
11		AppEnv        string // "development" | "production"
12		BannedWords   string // comma-separated list of prohibited comment words
13	}
14	
15	// Load reads configuration from environment variables, applying defaults where
16	// a variable is absent or empty.
17	func Load() *Config {
18		return &Config{
19			Port:          envOrDefault("PORT", "3000"),
20			DBPath:        envOrDefault("DB_PATH", "data/portal.db"),
21			EncryptionKey: envOrDefault("ENCRYPTION_KEY", ""),
22			SessionSecret: envOrDefault("SESSION_SECRET", ""),
23			AppEnv:        envOrDefault("APP_ENV", "development"),
24			BannedWords:   envOrDefault("BANNED_WORDS", ""),
25		}
26	}
27	
28	// envOrDefault returns the value of the named environment variable, or
29	// fallback when the variable is unset or empty.
30	func envOrDefault(key, fallback string) string {
31		if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
32			return v
33		}
34		return fallback
35	}

1	package main
2	
3	import (
4		"errors"
5		"fmt"
6		"log"
7		"os"
8		"os/signal"
9		"strings"
10		"syscall"
11		"time"
12	
13		"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2"
14		fibercsrf "github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2/middleware/csrf"
15		"github.com/gofiber/template/html/v2"
16	
17		"w2t86/internal/config"
18		"w2t86/internal/crypto"
19		"w2t86/internal/db"
20		"w2t86/internal/handlers"
21		"w2t86/internal/middleware"
22		"w2t86/internal/observability"
23		"w2t86/internal/repository"
24		"w2t86/internal/scheduler"
25		"w2t86/internal/services"
26	)
27	
28	func main() {
29		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
30		// 1. Load configuration from environment / .env                       //
31		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
32		cfg := config.Load()
33	
34		// Initialize structured loggers as early as possible.
35		observability.Init(cfg.AppEnv)
36	
37		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
38		// 2. Open database (runs migrations automatically)                    //
39		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
40		database, err := db.Open(cfg.DBPath)
41		if err != nil {
42			log.Fatalf("main: open db: %v", err)
43		}
44		defer database.Close()
45	
46		// Verify the default admin credential at startup — this is the authoritative
47		// runtime proof that the seeded hash and the documented password are in sync.
48		// Using crypto.CheckPassword (bcrypt) rather than string equality confirms
49		// the credential is actually usable, not just that the hash text matches.
50		const (
51			defaultAdminPassword = "ChangeMe123!"
52			defaultAdminHash     = "$2a$12$fMPISK6tAC1XLVM3JdJQDuB/CrXgdRM.LUPHHu4/VxS/vzihnYyQ."
53		)
54		var storedHash string
55		if err := database.QueryRow(`SELECT password_hash FROM users WHERE username = 'admin' LIMIT 1`).Scan(&storedHash); err == nil {
56			if storedHash == defaultAdminHash {
57				// Verify the hash is genuinely usable (proves the credential works).
58				if crypto.CheckPassword(storedHash, defaultAdminPassword) {
59					log.Println("WARNING: admin account is using the default password (ChangeMe123!). Change it immediately via the admin panel.")
60				} else {
61					log.Println("ERROR: admin account has the default hash but bcrypt check failed — seed data may be corrupt.")
62				}
63			}
64		}
65	
66		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
67		// 3. Repositories                                                     //
68		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
69		userRepo         := repository.NewUserRepository(database)
70		sessionRepo      := repository.NewSessionRepository(database)
71		materialRepo     := repository.NewMaterialRepository(database)
72		engagementRepo   := repository.NewEngagementRepository(database)
73		orderRepo        := repository.NewOrderRepository(database)
74		distributionRepo := repository.NewDistributionRepository(database)
75		messagingRepo    := repository.NewMessagingRepository(database)
76		moderationRepo   := repository.NewModerationRepository(database)
77		analyticsRepo    := repository.NewAnalyticsRepository(database)
78		adminRepo        := repository.NewAdminRepository(database)
79	
80		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
81		// 4. Services                                                         //
82		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
83		authService         := services.NewAuthService(userRepo, sessionRepo, cfg)
84		materialService     := services.NewMaterialService(materialRepo, engagementRepo)
85		if cfg.BannedWords != "" {
86			words := strings.Split(cfg.BannedWords, ",")
87			for i, w := range words {
88				words[i] = strings.TrimSpace(w)
89			}
90			materialService.SetWordFilter(services.NewWordFilter(words))
91		}
92		orderService        := services.NewOrderService(orderRepo, materialRepo)
93		distributionService := services.NewDistributionService(distributionRepo, orderRepo, materialRepo)
94		messagingService    := services.NewMessagingService(messagingRepo)
95		moderationService   := services.NewModerationService(moderationRepo)
96		analyticsService    := services.NewAnalyticsService(analyticsRepo)
97		adminService        := services.NewAdminService(adminRepo, userRepo, materialRepo)
98	
99		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
100		// 5. Handlers                                                         //
101		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
102		authHandler         := handlers.NewAuthHandler(authService)
103		materialHandler     := handlers.NewMaterialHandler(materialService)
104		orderHandler        := handlers.NewOrderHandler(orderService)
105		distributionHandler := handlers.NewDistributionHandler(distributionService)
106		messagingHandler    := handlers.NewMessagingHandler(messagingService)
107		moderationHandler   := handlers.NewModerationHandler(moderationService, messagingService)
108		analyticsHandler    := handlers.NewAnalyticsHandler(analyticsService)
109		adminHandler        := handlers.NewAdminHandler(adminService, authService)
110	
111		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
112		// 6. Middleware / rate limiters                                       //
113		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
114		authMiddleware := middleware.NewAuthMiddleware(sessionRepo, userRepo)
115	
116		// Login rate-limiter: max 10 attempts per minute per IP.
117		loginLimiter   := middleware.NewRateLimiter(10, time.Minute)
118		loginRateLimit := loginLimiter.Middleware(func(c *fiber.Ctx) string {
119			return c.IP()
120		})
121	
122		// Comment rate-limiter: 5 per 10 minutes per authenticated user.
123		commentRateLimit := middleware.CommentRateLimit()
124	
125		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
126		// 7. Scheduler                                                        //
127		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
128		sched := scheduler.NewOrderScheduler(database)
129		sched.Start()
130		defer sched.Stop()
131	
132		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
133		// 8. Template engine                                                  //
134		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
135		engine := html.New("web/templates", ".html")
136		if cfg.AppEnv == "development" {
137			engine.Reload(true) // re-parse templates on every request in dev
138		}
139		engine.AddFunc("mul", func(a, b float64) float64 { return a * b })
140		engine.AddFunc("add", func(a, b int) int { return a + b })
141		engine.AddFunc("sub", func(a, b float64) float64 { return a - b })
142		engine.AddFunc("div", func(a, b float64) float64 {
143			if b == 0 {
144				return 0
145			}
146			return a / b
147		})
148		engine.AddFunc("float64", func(v interface{}) float64 {
149			switch n := v.(type) {
150			case int:
151				return float64(n)
152			case int64:
153				return float64(n)
154			case float64:
155				return n
156			}
157			return 0
158		})
159		engine.AddFunc("dict", func(values ...interface{}) (map[string]interface{}, error) {
160			if len(values)%2 != 0 {
161				return nil, fmt.Errorf("dict requires even number of arguments")
162			}
163			d := make(map[string]interface{}, len(values)/2)
164			for i := 0; i < len(values); i += 2 {
165				key, ok := values[i].(string)
166				if !ok {
167					return nil, fmt.Errorf("dict keys must be strings")
168				}
169				d[key] = values[i+1]
170			}
171			return d, nil
172		})
173		engine.AddFunc("dec", func(n int) int { return n - 1 })
174		engine.AddFunc("inc", func(n int) int { return n + 1 })
175		engine.AddFunc("deref", func(v interface{}) interface{} { return v })
176		engine.AddFunc("hourRange", func() []int {
177			hours := make([]int, 24)
178			for i := range hours {
179				hours[i] = i
180			}
181			return hours
182		})
183	
184		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
185		// 9. Fiber app                                                        //
186		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
187		app := fiber.New(fiber.Config{
188			Views:       engine,
189			ViewsLayout: "layouts/main",
190			ErrorHandler: func(c *fiber.Ctx, err error) error {
191				code := fiber.StatusInternalServerError
192				var fe *fiber.Error
193				if errors.As(err, &fe) {
194					code = fe.Code
195				}
196				observability.App.Error("unhandled fiber error", "error", err, "path", c.Path(), "method", c.Method(), "ip", c.IP())
197				if c.Get("HX-Request") == "true" {
198					return c.Status(code).Render("partials/error_inline", fiber.Map{"Msg": "An unexpected error occurred."})
199				}
200				return c.Status(code).JSON(struct {
201					Code int    `json:"code"`
202					Msg  string `json:"msg"`
203				}{Code: code, Msg: "An unexpected error occurred."})
204			},
205		})
206	
207		// Serve static assets from web/static at /static.
208		app.Static("/static", "web/static")
209	
210		// Observability middleware — register before all routes.
211		app.Use(observability.RequestID())
212		app.Use(observability.RequestLogger())
213	
214		// Health check — used by Docker HEALTHCHECK and load balancers.
215		app.Get("/health", func(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
216			if err := database.PingContext(c.Context()); err != nil {
217				return c.Status(503).JSON(fiber.Map{"status": "unhealthy", "error": err.Error()})
218			}
219			return c.JSON(fiber.Map{
220				"status": "ok",
221				"uptime": observability.M.Uptime(),
222			})
223		})
224	
225		// Metrics endpoint — admin-only; exposes internal counters.
226		// Registered here but the handler itself re-checks the role so that
227		// the route ordering (before the session middleware) doesn't matter.
228		app.Get("/metrics", authMiddleware.RequireAuth(), middleware.RequireRole("admin"), func(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
229			return c.JSON(observability.M.ToJSON())
230		})
231	
232		// Inject encryption key into every request context so admin handlers
233		// can decrypt custom fields.
234		app.Use(func(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
235			c.Locals("enc_key", cfg.EncryptionKey)
236			return c.Next()
237		})
238	
239		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
240		// 10. Routes                                                          //
241		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
242	
243		// Root redirect.
244		app.Get("/", func(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
245			return c.Redirect("/dashboard", fiber.StatusFound)
246		})
247	
248		// ------------------------------------------------------------------
249		// Public routes (no auth required)
250		// ------------------------------------------------------------------
251		app.Get("/login",  authHandler.LoginPage)
252		app.Post("/login", loginRateLimit, authHandler.Login)
253	
254		// Public shared favorites list.
255		app.Get("/share/:token", materialHandler.SharedList)
256	
257		// ------------------------------------------------------------------
258		// Protected routes (RequireAuth)
259		// ------------------------------------------------------------------
260		protected := app.Group("", authMiddleware.RequireAuth())
261	
262		// CSRF protection for all state-changing endpoints on the protected group.
263		// Token is stored in a JS-readable cookie; HTMX reads it and sends it as
264		// the X-Csrf-Token header on every non-GET request (see base.html).
265		protected.Use(fibercsrf.New(fibercsrf.Config{
266			KeyLookup:      "header:X-Csrf-Token",
267			CookieName:     "csrf_token",
268			CookieHTTPOnly: false,
269			CookieSameSite: "Strict",
270		}))
271	
272		// Logout
273		protected.Post("/logout", authHandler.Logout)
274	
275		// Dashboard — role-based redirect.
276		protected.Get("/dashboard", func(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
277			user := middleware.GetUser(c)
278			switch user.Role {
279			case "admin":
280				return c.Redirect("/dashboard/admin", fiber.StatusFound)
281			case "instructor":
282				return c.Redirect("/dashboard/instructor", fiber.StatusFound)
283			default:
284				return c.Render("dashboard", fiber.Map{
285					"Title": "Dashboard",
286					"User":  user,
287				}, "layouts/base")
288			}
289		})
290	
291		// Materials catalog
292		protected.Get("/materials",        materialHandler.ListPage)
293		protected.Get("/materials/search", materialHandler.SearchPartial)
294		protected.Get("/materials/:id",    materialHandler.DetailPage)
295	
296		protected.Post("/materials/:id/rate",     materialHandler.Rate)
297		protected.Post("/materials/:id/comments", commentRateLimit, materialHandler.AddComment)
298		protected.Post("/comments/:id/report",    materialHandler.ReportComment)
299	
300		// Favorites — handler methods use :id for the list ID
301		protected.Get("/favorites",                              materialHandler.FavoritesList)
302		protected.Post("/favorites",                             materialHandler.CreateFavoritesList)

426			if err := app.Listen(addr); err != nil {
427				log.Printf("main: server error: %v", err)

28	func main() {
29		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
30		// 1. Load configuration from environment / .env                       //
31		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
32		cfg := config.Load()
33	
34		// Initialize structured loggers as early as possible.
35		observability.Init(cfg.AppEnv)
36	
37		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
38		// 2. Open database (runs migrations automatically)                    //
39		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
40		database, err := db.Open(cfg.DBPath)
41		if err != nil {
42			log.Fatalf("main: open db: %v", err)
43		}
44		defer database.Close()
45	
46		// Verify the default admin credential at startup — this is the authoritative
47		// runtime proof that the seeded hash and the documented password are in sync.
48		// Using crypto.CheckPassword (bcrypt) rather than string equality confirms
49		// the credential is actually usable, not just that the hash text matches.
50		const (
51			defaultAdminPassword = "ChangeMe123!"
52			defaultAdminHash     = "$2a$12$fMPISK6tAC1XLVM3JdJQDuB/CrXgdRM.LUPHHu4/VxS/vzihnYyQ."
53		)
54		var storedHash string
55		if err := database.QueryRow(`SELECT password_hash FROM users WHERE username = 'admin' LIMIT 1`).Scan(&storedHash); err == nil {
56			if storedHash == defaultAdminHash {
57				// Verify the hash is genuinely usable (proves the credential works).
58				if crypto.CheckPassword(storedHash, defaultAdminPassword) {
59					log.Println("WARNING: admin account is using the default password (ChangeMe123!). Change it immediately via the admin panel.")
60				} else {
61					log.Println("ERROR: admin account has the default hash but bcrypt check failed — seed data may be corrupt.")
62				}
63			}
64		}
65	
66		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
67		// 3. Repositories                                                     //
68		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
69		userRepo         := repository.NewUserRepository(database)
70		sessionRepo      := repository.NewSessionRepository(database)
71		materialRepo     := repository.NewMaterialRepository(database)
72		engagementRepo   := repository.NewEngagementRepository(database)
73		orderRepo        := repository.NewOrderRepository(database)
74		distributionRepo := repository.NewDistributionRepository(database)
75		messagingRepo    := repository.NewMessagingRepository(database)
76		moderationRepo   := repository.NewModerationRepository(database)
77		analyticsRepo    := repository.NewAnalyticsRepository(database)
78		adminRepo        := repository.NewAdminRepository(database)

80		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //

83		authService         := services.NewAuthService(userRepo, sessionRepo, cfg)
84		materialService     := services.NewMaterialService(materialRepo, engagementRepo)

89			}
90			materialService.SetWordFilter(services.NewWordFilter(words))
91		}
92		orderService        := services.NewOrderService(orderRepo, materialRepo)

116		// Login rate-limiter: max 10 attempts per minute per IP.

134		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
135		engine := html.New("web/templates", ".html")
136		if cfg.AppEnv == "development" {
137			engine.Reload(true) // re-parse templates on every request in dev
138		}
139		engine.AddFunc("mul", func(a, b float64) float64 { return a * b })
140		engine.AddFunc("add", func(a, b int) int { return a + b })
141		engine.AddFunc("sub", func(a, b float64) float64 { return a - b })
142		engine.AddFunc("div", func(a, b float64) float64 {
143			if b == 0 {
144				return 0
145			}
146			return a / b
147		})
148		engine.AddFunc("float64", func(v interface{}) float64 {
149			switch n := v.(type) {
150			case int:
151				return float64(n)
152			case int64:
153				return float64(n)
154			case float64:
155				return n
156			}
157			return 0
158		})
159		engine.AddFunc("dict", func(values ...interface{}) (map[string]interface{}, error) {
160			if len(values)%2 != 0 {
161				return nil, fmt.Errorf("dict requires even number of arguments")
162			}
163			d := make(map[string]interface{}, len(values)/2)
164			for i := 0; i < len(values); i += 2 {
165				key, ok := values[i].(string)
166				if !ok {
167					return nil, fmt.Errorf("dict keys must be strings")
168				}
169				d[key] = values[i+1]
170			}
171			return d, nil
172		})
173		engine.AddFunc("dec", func(n int) int { return n - 1 })
174		engine.AddFunc("inc", func(n int) int { return n + 1 })
175		engine.AddFunc("deref", func(v interface{}) interface{} { return v })
176		engine.AddFunc("hourRange", func() []int {
177			hours := make([]int, 24)
178			for i := range hours {
179				hours[i] = i
180			}
181			return hours
182		})

186		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //

189			ViewsLayout: "layouts/main",
190			ErrorHandler: func(c *fiber.Ctx, err error) error {
191				code := fiber.StatusInternalServerError
192				var fe *fiber.Error
193				if errors.As(err, &fe) {
194					code = fe.Code
195				}
196				observability.App.Error("unhandled fiber error", "error", err, "path", c.Path(), "method", c.Method(), "ip", c.IP())
197				if c.Get("HX-Request") == "true" {
198					return c.Status(code).Render("partials/error_inline", fiber.Map{"Msg": "An unexpected error occurred."})
199				}
200				return c.Status(code).JSON(struct {
201					Code int    `json:"code"`
202					Msg  string `json:"msg"`
203				}{Code: code, Msg: "An unexpected error occurred."})
204			},

386		adminGroup.Get("/admin/materials/:id/edit",     materialHandler.EditMaterialForm)
387		adminGroup.Put("/admin/materials/:id",          materialHandler.UpdateMaterial)
388		adminGroup.Delete("/admin/materials/:id",       materialHandler.DeleteMaterial)
389	
390		// User management (admin)
391		adminGroup.Get("/admin/users",                      adminHandler.ListUsers)
392		adminGroup.Get("/admin/users/new",                  adminHandler.NewUserForm)
393		adminGroup.Post("/admin/users",                     adminHandler.CreateUser)
394		adminGroup.Get("/admin/users/:id",                  adminHandler.UserProfile)
395		adminGroup.Post("/admin/users/:id/role",            adminHandler.UpdateRole)
396		adminGroup.Post("/admin/users/:id/unlock",          adminHandler.UnlockUser)
397		adminGroup.Get("/admin/users/:id/fields",           adminHandler.CustomFieldsPage)
398		adminGroup.Post("/admin/users/:id/fields",          adminHandler.SetCustomField)
399		adminGroup.Delete("/admin/users/:id/fields/:name",  adminHandler.DeleteCustomField)
400	
401		// Duplicate detection
402		adminGroup.Get("/admin/duplicates",        adminHandler.DuplicatesPage)
403		adminGroup.Post("/admin/duplicates/merge", adminHandler.MergeUsers)
404	
405		// Audit log
406		adminGroup.Get("/admin/audit",                            adminHandler.AuditLogPage)
407		adminGroup.Get("/admin/audit/:entityType/:entityID",      adminHandler.EntityAuditLog)
408	
409		// Analytics (admin)
410		adminGroup.Get("/analytics/map",             analyticsHandler.MapPage)
411		adminGroup.Get("/analytics/map/data",        analyticsHandler.MapData)
412		adminGroup.Post("/analytics/map/compute",    analyticsHandler.ComputeGrid)
413		adminGroup.Get("/analytics/export/orders",   analyticsHandler.ExportOrders)
414		adminGroup.Get("/analytics/export/distribution", analyticsHandler.ExportDistribution)
415		adminGroup.Get("/analytics/kpi/:name",       analyticsHandler.KPIHistory)
416	
417		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
418		// 11. Graceful shutdown                                               //
419		// ------------------------------------------------------------------ //
420		quit := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
421		signal.Notify(quit, os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
422	
423		go func() {
424			addr := ":" + cfg.Port
425			log.Printf("main: listening on %s (env=%s)", addr, cfg.AppEnv)
426			if err := app.Listen(addr); err != nil {
427				log.Printf("main: server error: %v", err)
428			}
429		}()
430	
431		<-quit
432		log.Println("main: shutting down...")
433	
434		if err := app.Shutdown(); err != nil {
435			log.Printf("main: shutdown error: %v", err)
436		}
437	
438		log.Println("main: shutdown complete")
439	}

1	package handlers
2	
3	import (
4		"fmt"
5	
6		"github.com/gofiber/fiber/v2"
7	
8		"w2t86/internal/middleware"
9		"w2t86/internal/observability"
10		"w2t86/internal/services"
11	)
12	
13	// AnalyticsHandler handles all analytics, KPI, geospatial, and export routes.
14	type AnalyticsHandler struct {
15		analyticsService *services.AnalyticsService
16	}
17	
18	// NewAnalyticsHandler creates an AnalyticsHandler backed by the given service.
19	func NewAnalyticsHandler(as *services.AnalyticsService) *AnalyticsHandler {
20		return &AnalyticsHandler{analyticsService: as}
21	}
22	
23	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
24	// Dashboard routes
25	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
26	
27	// AdminDashboard handles GET /dashboard/admin
28	// Renders the admin analytics dashboard with full KPI data.
29	func (h *AnalyticsHandler) AdminDashboard(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
30		user := middleware.GetUser(c)
31	
32		data, err := h.analyticsService.AdminDashboardData()
33		if err != nil {
34			return internalErr(c, observability.App, "load admin dashboard data failed", err, "user_id", user.ID)
35		}
36	
37		// Compute total and pending counts for the KPI cards.
38		total := 0
39		for _, n := range data.OrdersByStatus {
40			total += n
41		}
42		pending := data.OrdersByStatus["pending_payment"] + data.OrdersByStatus["pending_shipment"]
43	
44		return c.Render("analytics/admin_dashboard", fiber.Map{
45			"Title":           "Admin Dashboard",
46			"User":            user,
47			"ActivePage":      "analytics",
48			"Dashboard":       data,
49			"TotalOrders":     total,
50			"PendingOrders":   pending,
51			"FulfillmentRate": fmt.Sprintf("%.1f", data.FulfillmentRate),
52			"ReturnRate":      fmt.Sprintf("%.1f", data.ReturnRate),
53			"ActiveUsers":     data.ActiveUsers,
54			"Inventory":       data.Inventory,
55			"TopMaterials":    data.TopMaterials,
56			"OrdersByStatus":  data.OrdersByStatus,
57			"GMV":             fmt.Sprintf("%.2f", data.GMV),
58			"AOV":             fmt.Sprintf("%.2f", data.AOV),
59			"ConversionRate":  fmt.Sprintf("%.1f", data.ConversionRate),
60			"RepeatPurchase":  fmt.Sprintf("%.1f", data.RepeatPurchase),
61			"Funnel":          data.Funnel,
62		}, "layouts/base")
63	}
64	
65	// InstructorDashboard handles GET /dashboard/instructor
66	// Renders the instructor analytics dashboard.
67	func (h *AnalyticsHandler) InstructorDashboard(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
68		user := middleware.GetUser(c)
69	
70		data, err := h.analyticsService.InstructorDashboardData(user.ID)
71		if err != nil {
72			return internalErr(c, observability.App, "load instructor dashboard data failed", err, "user_id", user.ID)
73		}
74	
75		return c.Render("analytics/instructor_dashboard", fiber.Map{
76			"Title":            "Instructor Dashboard",
77			"User":             user,
78			"ActivePage":       "analytics",
79			"CourseStats":      data.CourseStats,
80			"PendingApprovals": data.PendingApprovals,
81		}, "layouts/base")
82	}
83	
84	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
85	// Geospatial routes
86	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
87	
88	// MapPage handles GET /analytics/map
89	// Renders the full-screen Leaflet map page.
90	func (h *AnalyticsHandler) MapPage(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
91		user := middleware.GetUser(c)
92		return c.Render("analytics/map", fiber.Map{
93			"Title":      "Geospatial Map",
94			"User":       user,
95			"ActivePage": "analytics",
96		}, "layouts/base")
97	}
98	
99	// MapData handles GET /analytics/map/data
100	// Returns MapData as JSON for consumption by Leaflet/map.js.
101	func (h *AnalyticsHandler) MapData(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
102		layerType := c.Query("layer", "")
103	
104		mapData, err := h.analyticsService.GetMapData(layerType)
105		if err != nil {
106			observability.App.Error("get map data failed", "error", err, "path", c.Path(), "method", c.Method(), "layer", layerType)
107			return c.Status(fiber.StatusInternalServerError).JSON(APIError{
108				Code: fiber.StatusInternalServerError,
109				Msg:  "An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.",
110			})
111		}
112	
113		return c.JSON(fiber.Map{
114			"locations":  mapData.Locations,
115			"aggregates": mapData.Aggregates,
116			"geojson":    mapData.GeoJSON,
117		})
118	}
119	
120	// ComputeGrid handles POST /analytics/map/compute
121	// Triggers grid aggregation computation.
122	func (h *AnalyticsHandler) ComputeGrid(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
123		layerType := c.FormValue("layer", "")
124		metric := c.FormValue("metric", "count")
125		gridSizeKm := float64(10)
126		if gs := c.FormValue("grid_size_km"); gs != "" {
127			if v, err := fmt.Sscanf(gs, "%f", &gridSizeKm); err != nil || v == 0 {
128				gridSizeKm = 10
129			}
130		}
131	
132		if err := h.analyticsService.ComputeGrid(layerType, metric, gridSizeKm); err != nil {
133			observability.App.Error("compute grid failed", "error", err, "path", c.Path(), "method", c.Method(), "layer", layerType, "metric", metric)
134			return c.Status(fiber.StatusInternalServerError).JSON(APIError{
135				Code: fiber.StatusInternalServerError,
136				Msg:  "An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.",
137			})
138		}
139	
140		return c.JSON(fiber.Map{"status": "ok"})
141	}
142	
143	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
144	// Export routes
145	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
146	
147	// ExportOrders handles GET /analytics/export/orders
148	// Streams a CSV file download with PII-masked order data.
149	func (h *AnalyticsHandler) ExportOrders(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
150		user := middleware.GetUser(c)
151		status := c.Query("status")
152		dateFrom := c.Query("date_from")
153		dateTo := c.Query("date_to")
154	
155		observability.App.Info("orders export requested",
156			"actor_id", user.ID, "role", user.Role,
157			"status_filter", status, "date_from", dateFrom, "date_to", dateTo,
158			"ip", c.IP())
159	
160		csvBytes, err := h.analyticsService.ExportOrdersCSV(status, dateFrom, dateTo)
161		if err != nil {
162			return internalErr(c, observability.App, "export orders CSV failed", err,
163				"actor_id", user.ID, "status_filter", status)
164		}
165	
166		c.Set("Content-Type", "text/csv")
167		c.Set("Content-Disposition", `attachment; filename="orders_export.csv"`)
168		return c.Send(csvBytes)
169	}
170	
171	// ExportDistribution handles GET /analytics/export/distribution
172	// Streams a CSV file download with PII-masked distribution event data.
173	func (h *AnalyticsHandler) ExportDistribution(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
174		user := middleware.GetUser(c)
175		dateFrom := c.Query("date_from")
176		dateTo := c.Query("date_to")
177	
178		observability.App.Info("distribution export requested",
179			"actor_id", user.ID, "role", user.Role,
180			"date_from", dateFrom, "date_to", dateTo,
181			"ip", c.IP())
182	
183		csvBytes, err := h.analyticsService.ExportDistributionCSV(dateFrom, dateTo)
184		if err != nil {
185			return internalErr(c, observability.App, "export distribution CSV failed", err,
186				"actor_id", user.ID, "date_from", dateFrom, "date_to", dateTo)
187		}
188	
189		c.Set("Content-Type", "text/csv")
190		c.Set("Content-Disposition", `attachment; filename="distribution_export.csv"`)
191		return c.Send(csvBytes)
192	}
193	
194	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
195	// Dashboard stat card API  (GET /api/stats/:stat)
196	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
197	
198	// DashboardStat handles GET /api/stats/:stat — returns a card-body HTML
199	// partial used by HTMX to populate dashboard KPI cards on page load.
200	func (h *AnalyticsHandler) DashboardStat(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
201		user := middleware.GetUser(c)
202		stat := c.Params("stat")
203	
204		card, err := h.analyticsService.DashboardStat(stat, user.ID)
205		if err != nil {
206			observability.App.Warn("dashboard stat failed", "stat", stat, "user_id", user.ID, "error", err)
207			// Return a graceful placeholder rather than a hard error so the card
208			// stays visible.
209			card = &services.DashboardStatCard{Count: 0, Label: stat, Icon: "bar-chart", Color: "secondary"}
210		}
211	
212		return c.Render("partials/stat_card_body", fiber.Map{
213			"Count": card.Count,
214			"Label": card.Label,
215			"Icon":  card.Icon,
216			"Color": card.Color,
217		})
218	}
219	
220	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
221	// KPI history route
222	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
223	
224	// KPIHistory handles GET /analytics/kpi/:name
225	// Returns KPI snapshot history as JSON for charts.
226	func (h *AnalyticsHandler) KPIHistory(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
227		name := c.Params("name")
228		dimension := c.Query("dimension", "")
229		limit := c.QueryInt("limit", 30)
230	
231		history, err := h.analyticsService.GetKPIHistory(name, dimension, limit)
232		if err != nil {
233			observability.App.Error("get KPI history failed", "error", err, "path", c.Path(), "method", c.Method(), "kpi_name", name)
234			return c.Status(fiber.StatusInternalServerError).JSON(APIError{
235				Code: fiber.StatusInternalServerError,
236				Msg:  "An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.",
237			})
238		}
239	
240		return c.JSON(fiber.Map{
241			"metric":    name,
242			"dimension": dimension,
243			"history":   history,
244		})
245	}
246

138		}
139	
140		return c.JSON(fiber.Map{"status": "ok"})
141	}
142	
143	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
144	// Export routes
145	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
146	
147	// ExportOrders handles GET /analytics/export/orders
148	// Streams a CSV file download with PII-masked order data.
149	func (h *AnalyticsHandler) ExportOrders(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
150		user := middleware.GetUser(c)
151		status := c.Query("status")
152		dateFrom := c.Query("date_from")
153		dateTo := c.Query("date_to")
154	
155		observability.App.Info("orders export requested",
156			"actor_id", user.ID, "role", user.Role,
157			"status_filter", status, "date_from", dateFrom, "date_to", dateTo,
158			"ip", c.IP())
159	
160		csvBytes, err := h.analyticsService.ExportOrdersCSV(status, dateFrom, dateTo)
161		if err != nil {
162			return internalErr(c, observability.App, "export orders CSV failed", err,
163				"actor_id", user.ID, "status_filter", status)
164		}
165	
166		c.Set("Content-Type", "text/csv")
167		c.Set("Content-Disposition", `attachment; filename="orders_export.csv"`)
168		return c.Send(csvBytes)
169	}
170	
171	// ExportDistribution handles GET /analytics/export/distribution
172	// Streams a CSV file download with PII-masked distribution event data.
173	func (h *AnalyticsHandler) ExportDistribution(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
174		user := middleware.GetUser(c)
175		dateFrom := c.Query("date_from")
176		dateTo := c.Query("date_to")
177	
178		observability.App.Info("distribution export requested",
179			"actor_id", user.ID, "role", user.Role,
180			"date_from", dateFrom, "date_to", dateTo,

186				"actor_id", user.ID, "date_from", dateFrom, "date_to", dateTo)
187		}

190		c.Set("Content-Disposition", `attachment; filename="distribution_export.csv"`)
191		return c.Send(csvBytes)
192	}
193	
194	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
195	// Dashboard stat card API  (GET /api/stats/:stat)
196	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
197	
198	// DashboardStat handles GET /api/stats/:stat — returns a card-body HTML
199	// partial used by HTMX to populate dashboard KPI cards on page load.
200	func (h *AnalyticsHandler) DashboardStat(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
201		user := middleware.GetUser(c)
202		stat := c.Params("stat")
203	
204		card, err := h.analyticsService.DashboardStat(stat, user.ID)
205		if err != nil {
206			observability.App.Warn("dashboard stat failed", "stat", stat, "user_id", user.ID, "error", err)
207			// Return a graceful placeholder rather than a hard error so the card
208			// stays visible.
209			card = &services.DashboardStatCard{Count: 0, Label: stat, Icon: "bar-chart", Color: "secondary"}
210		}
211	
212		return c.Render("partials/stat_card_body", fiber.Map{
213			"Count": card.Count,
214			"Label": card.Label,
215			"Icon":  card.Icon,
216			"Color": card.Color,
217		})
218	}
219	
220	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
221	// KPI history route
222	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
223	
224	// KPIHistory handles GET /analytics/kpi/:name
225	// Returns KPI snapshot history as JSON for charts.
226	func (h *AnalyticsHandler) KPIHistory(c *fiber.Ctx) error {
227		name := c.Params("name")
228		dimension := c.Query("dimension", "")
229		limit := c.QueryInt("limit", 30)
230	
231		history, err := h.analyticsService.GetKPIHistory(name, dimension, limit)
232		if err != nil {
233			observability.App.Error("get KPI history failed", "error", err, "path", c.Path(), "method", c.Method(), "kpi_name", name)
234			return c.Status(fiber.StatusInternalServerError).JSON(APIError{
235				Code: fiber.StatusInternalServerError,
236				Msg:  "An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.",
237			})
238		}
239	
240		return c.JSON(fiber.Map{
241			"metric":    name,
242			"dimension": dimension,
243			"history":   history,
244		})
245	}

1	package repository
2	
3	import (
4		"crypto/rand"
5		"database/sql"

7		"errors"
8		"fmt"
9		"time"
10	
11		"w2t86/internal/models"
12	)
13	
14	// ErrShareExpired is returned when a share token exists but has passed its expiry time.
15	var ErrShareExpired = errors.New("share link has expired")
16	
17	// EngagementRepository provides database operations for browse history, ratings,
18	// comments, comment reports, and favorites lists/items.
19	type EngagementRepository struct {
20		db *sql.DB
21	}
22	
23	// NewEngagementRepository returns an EngagementRepository backed by the given database.
24	func NewEngagementRepository(db *sql.DB) *EngagementRepository {
25		return &EngagementRepository{db: db}
26	}
27	
28	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
29	// Browse history
30	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
31	
32	// RecordVisit inserts (or replaces) a browse history row for the given user + material.
33	func (r *EngagementRepository) RecordVisit(userID, materialID int64) error {
34		const q = `
35			INSERT INTO browse_history (user_id, material_id, visited_at)
36			VALUES (?, ?, datetime('now'))`
37		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, userID, materialID)
38		return err
39	}
40	
41	// GetHistory returns the most-recently visited materials for a user.
42	func (r *EngagementRepository) GetHistory(userID int64, limit int) ([]models.BrowseHistory, error) {
43		const q = `
44			SELECT id, user_id, material_id, visited_at
45			FROM   browse_history
46			WHERE  user_id = ?
47			ORDER  BY visited_at DESC
48			LIMIT  ?`
49	
50		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, userID, limit)
51		if err != nil {
52			return nil, err
53		}
54		defer rows.Close()
55	
56		var items []models.BrowseHistory
57		for rows.Next() {
58			var bh models.BrowseHistory
59			if err := rows.Scan(&bh.ID, &bh.UserID, &bh.MaterialID, &bh.VisitedAt); err != nil {
60				return nil, err
61			}
62			items = append(items, bh)
63		}
64		return items, rows.Err()
65	}
66	
67	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
68	// Ratings
69	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
70	
71	// UpsertRating inserts or updates the star rating for a material/user pair.
72	func (r *EngagementRepository) UpsertRating(materialID, userID int64, stars int) error {
73		const q = `
74			INSERT INTO ratings (material_id, user_id, stars, created_at)
75			VALUES (?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))
76			ON CONFLICT(material_id, user_id) DO UPDATE SET stars = excluded.stars`
77		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, materialID, userID, stars)
78		return err
79	}
80	
81	// GetRating returns the rating a specific user gave to a material, or nil if none.
82	func (r *EngagementRepository) GetRating(materialID, userID int64) (*models.Rating, error) {
83		const q = `
84			SELECT id, material_id, user_id, stars, created_at
85			FROM   ratings
86			WHERE  material_id = ? AND user_id = ?`
87	
88		row := r.db.QueryRow(q, materialID, userID)
89		rt := &models.Rating{}
90		err := row.Scan(&rt.ID, &rt.MaterialID, &rt.UserID, &rt.Stars, &rt.CreatedAt)
91		if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
92			return nil, nil
93		}
94		if err != nil {
95			return nil, err
96		}
97		return rt, nil
98	}
99	
100	// GetAverageRating returns the average star rating and total count for a material.
101	func (r *EngagementRepository) GetAverageRating(materialID int64) (float64, int, error) {
102		const q = `
103			SELECT COALESCE(AVG(CAST(stars AS REAL)), 0.0), COUNT(*)
104			FROM   ratings
105			WHERE  material_id = ?`
106	
107		var avg float64
108		var count int
109		err := r.db.QueryRow(q, materialID).Scan(&avg, &count)
110		return avg, count, err
111	}
112	
113	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
114	// Comments
115	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
116	
117	// CreateComment inserts a new comment and returns the populated model.
118	func (r *EngagementRepository) CreateComment(materialID, userID int64, body string, linkCount int) (*models.Comment, error) {
119		const q = `
120			INSERT INTO comments (material_id, user_id, body, link_count, status, report_count)
121			VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 'visible', 0)
122			RETURNING id, material_id, user_id, body, link_count, status, report_count, created_at, updated_at`
123	
124		row := r.db.QueryRow(q, materialID, userID, body, linkCount)
125		c := &models.Comment{}
126		err := row.Scan(&c.ID, &c.MaterialID, &c.UserID, &c.Body, &c.LinkCount,
127			&c.Status, &c.ReportCount, &c.CreatedAt, &c.UpdatedAt)
128		if err != nil {
129			return nil, err
130		}
131		return c, nil
132	}
133	
134	// GetComments returns comments for a material, optionally including collapsed ones.
135	func (r *EngagementRepository) GetComments(materialID int64, includeCollapsed bool, limit, offset int) ([]models.Comment, error) {
136		var q string
137		if includeCollapsed {
138			q = `
139				SELECT id, material_id, user_id, body, link_count, status, report_count, created_at, updated_at
140				FROM   comments
141				WHERE  material_id = ?
142				ORDER  BY created_at DESC
143				LIMIT  ? OFFSET ?`
144		} else {
145			q = `
146				SELECT id, material_id, user_id, body, link_count, status, report_count, created_at, updated_at
147				FROM   comments
148				WHERE  material_id = ? AND status != 'removed'
149				ORDER  BY created_at DESC
150				LIMIT  ? OFFSET ?`
151		}
152	
153		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, materialID, limit, offset)
154		if err != nil {
155			return nil, err
156		}
157		defer rows.Close()
158	
159		var comments []models.Comment
160		for rows.Next() {
161			var c models.Comment
162			if err := rows.Scan(&c.ID, &c.MaterialID, &c.UserID, &c.Body, &c.LinkCount,
163				&c.Status, &c.ReportCount, &c.CreatedAt, &c.UpdatedAt); err != nil {
164				return nil, err
165			}
166			comments = append(comments, c)
167		}
168		return comments, rows.Err()
169	}
170	
171	// ReportComment records a report for a comment.
172	// After inserting the report, it counts unique reporters; if >= 3, the comment
173	// status is set to 'collapsed'.
174	func (r *EngagementRepository) ReportComment(commentID, reportedBy int64, reason string) error {
175		tx, err := r.db.Begin()
176		if err != nil {
177			return err
178		}
179		defer func() {
180			if err != nil {
181				_ = tx.Rollback()
182			}
183		}()
184	
185		const insertReport = `
186			INSERT OR IGNORE INTO comment_reports (comment_id, reported_by, reason, created_at)
187			VALUES (?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))`
188		_, err = tx.Exec(insertReport, commentID, reportedBy, reason)
189		if err != nil {
190			return err
191		}
192	
193		var uniqueReporters int
194		const countQ = `SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT reported_by) FROM comment_reports WHERE comment_id = ?`
195		err = tx.QueryRow(countQ, commentID).Scan(&uniqueReporters)
196		if err != nil {
197			return err
198		}
199	
200		if uniqueReporters >= 3 {
201			const collapseQ = `UPDATE comments SET status = 'collapsed', updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ? AND status = 'visible'`
202			if _, err = tx.Exec(collapseQ, commentID); err != nil {
203				return err
204			}
205		}
206	
207		return tx.Commit()
208	}
209	
210	// CountRecentComments returns the number of comments a user has posted since the given time.
211	func (r *EngagementRepository) CountRecentComments(userID int64, since time.Time) (int, error) {
212		const q = `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM comments WHERE user_id = ? AND created_at >= ?`
213		var count int
214		err := r.db.QueryRow(q, userID, since.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)).Scan(&count)
215		return count, err
216	}
217	
218	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
219	// Favorites
220	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
221	
222	// CreateList creates a new favorites list for a user.
223	func (r *EngagementRepository) CreateList(userID int64, name, visibility string) (*models.FavoritesList, error) {
224		const q = `
225			INSERT INTO favorites_lists (user_id, name, visibility)
226			VALUES (?, ?, ?)
227			RETURNING id, user_id, name, visibility, share_token, share_expires_at, created_at`
228	
229		row := r.db.QueryRow(q, userID, name, visibility)
230		fl := &models.FavoritesList{}
231		err := row.Scan(&fl.ID, &fl.UserID, &fl.Name, &fl.Visibility,
232			&fl.ShareToken, &fl.ShareExpiresAt, &fl.CreatedAt)
233		if err != nil {
234			return nil, err
235		}
236		return fl, nil
237	}
238	
239	// GetLists returns all favorites lists for a user.
240	func (r *EngagementRepository) GetLists(userID int64) ([]models.FavoritesList, error) {
241		const q = `
242			SELECT id, user_id, name, visibility, share_token, share_expires_at, created_at
243			FROM   favorites_lists
244			WHERE  user_id = ?
245			ORDER  BY created_at DESC`
246	
247		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, userID)
248		if err != nil {
249			return nil, err
250		}
251		defer rows.Close()
252	
253		var lists []models.FavoritesList
254		for rows.Next() {
255			var fl models.FavoritesList
256			if err := rows.Scan(&fl.ID, &fl.UserID, &fl.Name, &fl.Visibility,
257				&fl.ShareToken, &fl.ShareExpiresAt, &fl.CreatedAt); err != nil {
258				return nil, err
259			}
260			lists = append(lists, fl)
261		}
262		return lists, rows.Err()
263	}
264	
265	// GetListByID returns a single favorites list by its primary key.
266	func (r *EngagementRepository) GetListByID(id int64) (*models.FavoritesList, error) {
267		const q = `
268			SELECT id, user_id, name, visibility, share_token, share_expires_at, created_at
269			FROM   favorites_lists
270			WHERE  id = ?`
271	
272		row := r.db.QueryRow(q, id)
273		fl := &models.FavoritesList{}
274		err := row.Scan(&fl.ID, &fl.UserID, &fl.Name, &fl.Visibility,
275			&fl.ShareToken, &fl.ShareExpiresAt, &fl.CreatedAt)
276		if err != nil {
277			return nil, err
278		}
279		return fl, nil
280	}
281	
282	// GetListByShareToken returns the favorites list matching the given share token.
283	// Returns ErrShareExpired if the token exists but has expired, sql.ErrNoRows if
284	// no such token exists at all.
285	func (r *EngagementRepository) GetListByShareToken(token string) (*models.FavoritesList, error) {
286		const q = `
287			SELECT id, user_id, name, visibility, share_token, share_expires_at, created_at
288			FROM   favorites_lists
289			WHERE  share_token = ?`
290	
291		row := r.db.QueryRow(q, token)
292		fl := &models.FavoritesList{}
293		err := row.Scan(&fl.ID, &fl.UserID, &fl.Name, &fl.Visibility,
294			&fl.ShareToken, &fl.ShareExpiresAt, &fl.CreatedAt)
295		if err != nil {
296			return nil, err
297		}
298		// Check expiry in Go so callers can distinguish expired vs not-found.
299		if fl.ShareExpiresAt != nil {
300			if exp, parseErr := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, *fl.ShareExpiresAt); parseErr == nil {
301				if time.Now().After(exp) {
302					return nil, ErrShareExpired
303				}
304			}
305		}
306		return fl, nil
307	}
308	
309	// AddToList inserts a material into a favorites list (ignores duplicates).
310	func (r *EngagementRepository) AddToList(listID, materialID int64) error {
311		const q = `
312			INSERT OR IGNORE INTO favorites_items (list_id, material_id, added_at)
313			VALUES (?, ?, datetime('now'))`
314		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, listID, materialID)
315		return err
316	}
317	
318	// RemoveFromList deletes a material from a favorites list.
319	func (r *EngagementRepository) RemoveFromList(listID, materialID int64) error {
320		const q = `DELETE FROM favorites_items WHERE list_id = ? AND material_id = ?`
321		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, listID, materialID)
322		return err
323	}
324	
325	// GetListItems returns all items in a favorites list.
326	func (r *EngagementRepository) GetListItems(listID int64) ([]models.FavoritesItem, error) {
327		const q = `
328			SELECT id, list_id, material_id, added_at
329			FROM   favorites_items
330			WHERE  list_id = ?
331			ORDER  BY added_at DESC`
332	
333		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, listID)
334		if err != nil {
335			return nil, err
336		}
337		defer rows.Close()
338	
339		var items []models.FavoritesItem
340		for rows.Next() {
341			var fi models.FavoritesItem
342			if err := rows.Scan(&fi.ID, &fi.ListID, &fi.MaterialID, &fi.AddedAt); err != nil {
343				return nil, err
344			}
345			items = append(items, fi)
346		}
347		return items, rows.Err()
348	}
349	
350	// GenerateShareToken creates a crypto-random share token, stores it on the
351	// favorites list, and returns the raw token string.
352	func (r *EngagementRepository) GenerateShareToken(listID int64, expiresAt time.Time) (string, error) {
353		b := make([]byte, 24)
354		if _, err := rand.Read(b); err != nil {
355			return "", fmt.Errorf("repository: GenerateShareToken: rand: %w", err)
356		}
357		token := hex.EncodeToString(b)
358	
359		const q = `
360			UPDATE favorites_lists
361			SET    share_token = ?, share_expires_at = ?
362			WHERE  id = ?`
363		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, token, expiresAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339), listID)
364		if err != nil {
365			return "", err
366		}
367		return token, nil
368	}
369	
370	// UpdateListVisibility sets the visibility field on a favorites list.
371	func (r *EngagementRepository) UpdateListVisibility(listID int64, visibility string) error {
372		const q = `UPDATE favorites_lists SET visibility = ? WHERE id = ?`
373		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, visibility, listID)
374		return err
375	}

122			RETURNING id, material_id, user_id, body, link_count, status, report_count, created_at, updated_at`
123	
124		row := r.db.QueryRow(q, materialID, userID, body, linkCount)
125		c := &models.Comment{}
126		err := row.Scan(&c.ID, &c.MaterialID, &c.UserID, &c.Body, &c.LinkCount,
127			&c.Status, &c.ReportCount, &c.CreatedAt, &c.UpdatedAt)
128		if err != nil {
129			return nil, err
130		}
131		return c, nil
132	}
133	
134	// GetComments returns comments for a material, optionally including collapsed ones.
135	func (r *EngagementRepository) GetComments(materialID int64, includeCollapsed bool, limit, offset int) ([]models.Comment, error) {
136		var q string
137		if includeCollapsed {
138			q = `
139				SELECT id, material_id, user_id, body, link_count, status, report_count, created_at, updated_at
140				FROM   comments
141				WHERE  material_id = ?
142				ORDER  BY created_at DESC
143				LIMIT  ? OFFSET ?`
144		} else {
145			q = `
146				SELECT id, material_id, user_id, body, link_count, status, report_count, created_at, updated_at
147				FROM   comments
148				WHERE  material_id = ? AND status != 'removed'
149				ORDER  BY created_at DESC
150				LIMIT  ? OFFSET ?`
151		}
152	
153		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, materialID, limit, offset)
154		if err != nil {
155			return nil, err
156		}
157		defer rows.Close()
158	
159		var comments []models.Comment
160		for rows.Next() {
161			var c models.Comment
162			if err := rows.Scan(&c.ID, &c.MaterialID, &c.UserID, &c.Body, &c.LinkCount,
163				&c.Status, &c.ReportCount, &c.CreatedAt, &c.UpdatedAt); err != nil {
164				return nil, err
165			}
166			comments = append(comments, c)
167		}
168		return comments, rows.Err()
169	}

171	// ReportComment records a report for a comment.
172	// After inserting the report, it counts unique reporters; if >= 3, the comment
173	// status is set to 'collapsed'.
174	func (r *EngagementRepository) ReportComment(commentID, reportedBy int64, reason string) error {
175		tx, err := r.db.Begin()
176		if err != nil {
177			return err
178		}
179		defer func() {
180			if err != nil {
181				_ = tx.Rollback()
182			}
183		}()
184	
185		const insertReport = `
186			INSERT OR IGNORE INTO comment_reports (comment_id, reported_by, reason, created_at)
187			VALUES (?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))`
188		_, err = tx.Exec(insertReport, commentID, reportedBy, reason)
189		if err != nil {
190			return err
191		}
192	
193		var uniqueReporters int
194		const countQ = `SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT reported_by) FROM comment_reports WHERE comment_id = ?`
195		err = tx.QueryRow(countQ, commentID).Scan(&uniqueReporters)
196		if err != nil {
197			return err
198		}
199	
200		if uniqueReporters >= 3 {
201			const collapseQ = `UPDATE comments SET status = 'collapsed', updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ? AND status = 'visible'`
202			if _, err = tx.Exec(collapseQ, commentID); err != nil {
203				return err
204			}
205		}
206	
207		return tx.Commit()
208	}
209	
210	// CountRecentComments returns the number of comments a user has posted since the given time.
211	func (r *EngagementRepository) CountRecentComments(userID int64, since time.Time) (int, error) {
212		const q = `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM comments WHERE user_id = ? AND created_at >= ?`
213		var count int
214		err := r.db.QueryRow(q, userID, since.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339)).Scan(&count)
215		return count, err
216	}

282	// GetListByShareToken returns the favorites list matching the given share token.
283	// Returns ErrShareExpired if the token exists but has expired, sql.ErrNoRows if
284	// no such token exists at all.
285	func (r *EngagementRepository) GetListByShareToken(token string) (*models.FavoritesList, error) {
286		const q = `
287			SELECT id, user_id, name, visibility, share_token, share_expires_at, created_at
288			FROM   favorites_lists
289			WHERE  share_token = ?`
290	
291		row := r.db.QueryRow(q, token)
292		fl := &models.FavoritesList{}
293		err := row.Scan(&fl.ID, &fl.UserID, &fl.Name, &fl.Visibility,
294			&fl.ShareToken, &fl.ShareExpiresAt, &fl.CreatedAt)
295		if err != nil {
296			return nil, err
297		}
298		// Check expiry in Go so callers can distinguish expired vs not-found.
299		if fl.ShareExpiresAt != nil {
300			if exp, parseErr := time.Parse(time.RFC3339, *fl.ShareExpiresAt); parseErr == nil {
301				if time.Now().After(exp) {
302					return nil, ErrShareExpired
303				}
304			}
305		}
306		return fl, nil
307	}

350	// GenerateShareToken creates a crypto-random share token, stores it on the
351	// favorites list, and returns the raw token string.
352	func (r *EngagementRepository) GenerateShareToken(listID int64, expiresAt time.Time) (string, error) {
353		b := make([]byte, 24)
354		if _, err := rand.Read(b); err != nil {
355			return "", fmt.Errorf("repository: GenerateShareToken: rand: %w", err)
356		}
357		token := hex.EncodeToString(b)
358	
359		const q = `
360			UPDATE favorites_lists
361			SET    share_token = ?, share_expires_at = ?
362			WHERE  id = ?`
363		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, token, expiresAt.UTC().Format(time.RFC3339), listID)
364		if err != nil {
365			return "", err
366		}
367		return token, nil
368	}

372		const q = `UPDATE favorites_lists SET visibility = ? WHERE id = ?`
373		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, visibility, listID)
374		return err
375	}

1	package repository
2	
3	import (
4		"database/sql"
5		"fmt"
6		"strings"
7	
8		"w2t86/internal/models"
9	)
10	
11	// DistributionFilter holds optional filters for listing distribution events.
12	type DistributionFilter struct {
13		ScanID     string
14		MaterialID *int64
15		EventType  string
16		ActorID    *int64
17		DateFrom   string
18		DateTo     string
19	}
20	
21	// PendingIssue represents an order item that the clerk still needs to physically
22	// hand out.
23	type PendingIssue struct {
24		OrderID    int64
25		MaterialID int64
26		Title      string
27		Qty        int
28		Status     string
29		UserName   string
30	}
31	
32	// DistributionRepository provides database operations for distribution_events
33	// and related pick/pack queries.
34	type DistributionRepository struct {
35		db *sql.DB
36	}
37	
38	// NewDistributionRepository returns a DistributionRepository backed by db.
39	func NewDistributionRepository(db *sql.DB) *DistributionRepository {
40		return &DistributionRepository{db: db}
41	}
42	
43	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
44	// Write
45	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
46	
47	// RecordEvent inserts a new distribution_events row and returns the populated
48	// model (including the auto-assigned id and occurred_at).
49	func (r *DistributionRepository) RecordEvent(evt *models.DistributionEvent) (*models.DistributionEvent, error) {
50		const q = `
51			INSERT INTO distribution_events
52			            (order_id, material_id, qty, event_type, scan_id, actor_id,
53			             custody_from, custody_to, occurred_at)
54			VALUES      (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, datetime('now'))
55			RETURNING   id, order_id, material_id, qty, event_type, scan_id, actor_id,
56			            custody_from, custody_to, occurred_at`
57	
58		row := r.db.QueryRow(q,
59			evt.OrderID, evt.MaterialID, evt.Qty, evt.EventType, evt.ScanID,
60			evt.ActorID, evt.CustodyFrom, evt.CustodyTo,
61		)
62	
63		out := &models.DistributionEvent{}
64		if err := row.Scan(
65			&out.ID, &out.OrderID, &out.MaterialID, &out.Qty, &out.EventType,
66			&out.ScanID, &out.ActorID, &out.CustodyFrom, &out.CustodyTo, &out.OccurredAt,
67		); err != nil {
68			return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: RecordEvent: %w", err)
69		}
70		return out, nil
71	}
72	
73	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
74	// Read — per-order
75	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
76	
77	// GetByOrderID returns all distribution events for a given order, oldest first.
78	func (r *DistributionRepository) GetByOrderID(orderID int64) ([]models.DistributionEvent, error) {
79		const q = `
80			SELECT id, order_id, material_id, qty, event_type, scan_id, actor_id,
81			       custody_from, custody_to, occurred_at
82			FROM   distribution_events
83			WHERE  order_id = ?
84			ORDER  BY occurred_at ASC`
85	
86		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, orderID)
87		if err != nil {
88			return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: GetByOrderID: %w", err)
89		}
90		defer rows.Close()
91		return scanDistributionEvents(rows)
92	}
93	
94	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
95	// Read — ledger (filterable)
96	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
97	
98	// ListEvents returns a paginated, filtered view of all distribution events,
99	// newest first.
100	func (r *DistributionRepository) ListEvents(filters DistributionFilter, limit, offset int) ([]models.DistributionEvent, error) {
101		conditions := []string{"1=1"}
102		args := []interface{}{}
103	
104		if filters.ScanID != "" {
105			conditions = append(conditions, "scan_id = ?")
106			args = append(args, filters.ScanID)
107		}
108		if filters.MaterialID != nil {
109			conditions = append(conditions, "material_id = ?")
110			args = append(args, *filters.MaterialID)
111		}
112		if filters.EventType != "" {
113			conditions = append(conditions, "event_type = ?")
114			args = append(args, filters.EventType)
115		}
116		if filters.ActorID != nil {
117			conditions = append(conditions, "actor_id = ?")
118			args = append(args, *filters.ActorID)
119		}
120		if filters.DateFrom != "" {
121			conditions = append(conditions, "occurred_at >= ?")
122			args = append(args, filters.DateFrom)
123		}
124		if filters.DateTo != "" {
125			conditions = append(conditions, "occurred_at <= ?")
126			args = append(args, filters.DateTo)
127		}
128	
129		q := `
130			SELECT id, order_id, material_id, qty, event_type, scan_id, actor_id,
131			       custody_from, custody_to, occurred_at
132			FROM   distribution_events
133			WHERE  ` + strings.Join(conditions, " AND ") + `
134			ORDER  BY occurred_at DESC
135			LIMIT  ? OFFSET ?`
136	
137		args = append(args, limit, offset)
138		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, args...)
139		if err != nil {
140			return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: ListEvents: %w", err)
141		}
142		defer rows.Close()
143		return scanDistributionEvents(rows)
144	}
145	
146	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
147	// Read — custody chain
148	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
149	
150	// GetCustodyChain returns the full chronological event history for a specific
151	// physical copy identified by scan_id.
152	func (r *DistributionRepository) GetCustodyChain(scanID string) ([]models.DistributionEvent, error) {
153		const q = `
154			SELECT id, order_id, material_id, qty, event_type, scan_id, actor_id,
155			       custody_from, custody_to, occurred_at
156			FROM   distribution_events
157			WHERE  scan_id = ?
158			ORDER  BY occurred_at ASC`
159	
160		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, scanID)
161		if err != nil {
162			return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: GetCustodyChain: %w", err)
163		}
164		defer rows.Close()
165		return scanDistributionEvents(rows)
166	}
167	
168	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
169	// Read — pending issues / pick list
170	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
171	
172	// GetPendingIssues returns order items that have not yet been fully issued,
173	// where the parent order is in pending_shipment or in_transit status.
174	// It joins against materials for the title and users for the student name.
175	func (r *DistributionRepository) GetPendingIssues(limit, offset int) ([]PendingIssue, error) {
176		const q = `
177			SELECT  o.id            AS order_id,
178			        oi.material_id,
179			        m.title,
180			        oi.qty,
181			        o.status,
182			        u.username      AS user_name
183			FROM    order_items oi
184			JOIN    orders   o  ON o.id  = oi.order_id
185			JOIN    materials m  ON m.id  = oi.material_id
186			JOIN    users     u  ON u.id  = o.user_id
187			WHERE   o.status IN ('pending_shipment', 'in_transit')
188			  AND   oi.fulfillment_status = 'pending'
189			ORDER   BY o.created_at ASC
190			LIMIT   ? OFFSET ?`
191	
192		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, limit, offset)
193		if err != nil {
194			return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: GetPendingIssues: %w", err)
195		}
196		defer rows.Close()
197	
198		var out []PendingIssue
199		for rows.Next() {
200			var pi PendingIssue
201			if err := rows.Scan(
202				&pi.OrderID, &pi.MaterialID, &pi.Title,
203				&pi.Qty, &pi.Status, &pi.UserName,
204			); err != nil {
205				return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: GetPendingIssues: scan: %w", err)
206			}
207			out = append(out, pi)
208		}
209		return out, rows.Err()
210	}
211	
212	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
213	// Counts
214	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
215	
216	// CountBackorders returns the number of unresolved backorder records.
217	func (r *DistributionRepository) CountBackorders() (int, error) {
218		const q = `SELECT COUNT(*) FROM backorders WHERE resolved_at IS NULL`
219		var n int
220		if err := r.db.QueryRow(q).Scan(&n); err != nil {
221			return 0, fmt.Errorf("repository: CountBackorders: %w", err)
222		}
223		return n, nil
224	}
225	
226	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
227	// helpers
228	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
229	
230	func scanDistributionEvents(rows *sql.Rows) ([]models.DistributionEvent, error) {
231		var out []models.DistributionEvent
232		for rows.Next() {
233			var e models.DistributionEvent
234			if err := rows.Scan(
235				&e.ID, &e.OrderID, &e.MaterialID, &e.Qty, &e.EventType,
236				&e.ScanID, &e.ActorID, &e.CustodyFrom, &e.CustodyTo, &e.OccurredAt,
237			); err != nil {
238				return nil, fmt.Errorf("scanDistributionEvents: %w", err)
239			}
240			out = append(out, e)
241		}
242		return out, rows.Err()
243	}
