11	// TestModerationQueue_Empty verifies GET /moderation for a moderator returns
12	// a non-auth-error status. Template rendering may produce 500 if templates are
13	// not available; the critical invariant is non-403/401.
14	func TestModerationQueue_Empty(t *testing.T) {
15		app, db, cleanup := newTestApp(t)
16		defer cleanup()
17	
18		modCookie := loginAs(t, app, db, "moderator")
19	
20		resp := makeRequest(app, http.MethodGet, "/moderation", "", modCookie, "")
21		if resp.StatusCode == http.StatusUnauthorized {
22			t.Fatal("expected non-401 on /moderation with valid moderator session")
23		}
24		if resp.StatusCode == http.StatusForbidden {
25			t.Fatal("expected non-403 on /moderation for moderator")
26		}
27	}

29	// TestModerationQueue_RequiresModerator verifies that a student cannot access
30	// GET /moderation and receives 403 Forbidden.
31	func TestModerationQueue_RequiresModerator(t *testing.T) {
32		app, db, cleanup := newTestApp(t)
33		defer cleanup()
34	
35		studentCookie := loginAs(t, app, db, "student")
36	
37		resp := makeRequest(app, http.MethodGet, "/moderation", "", studentCookie, "")
38		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusForbidden {
39			t.Fatalf("expected 403 for student on /moderation, got %d", resp.StatusCode)
40		}
41	}
42	
43	// TestApproveComment verifies POST /moderation/:id/approve transitions a
44	// collapsed comment to active status in the DB.
45	func TestApproveComment(t *testing.T) {
46		app, db, cleanup := newTestApp(t)
47		defer cleanup()
48	
49		// Create a comment author.
50		author := createTestUser(t, db, "student")
51		mat := createTestMaterial(t, db)
52	
53		// Create comment and collapse it (set status directly).
54		engRepo := repository.NewEngagementRepository(db)
55		comment, err := engRepo.CreateComment(mat.ID, author.ID, "This needs review", 0)
56		if err != nil {
57			t.Fatalf("create comment: %v", err)
58		}
59	
60		// Set it to collapsed directly in DB.
61		if _, err := db.Exec(`UPDATE comments SET status = 'collapsed' WHERE id = ?`, comment.ID); err != nil {
62			t.Fatalf("set collapsed: %v", err)
63		}
64	
65		modCookie := loginAs(t, app, db, "moderator")
66	
67		resp := makeRequest(app, http.MethodPost,
68			fmt.Sprintf("/moderation/%d/approve", comment.ID),
69			"", modCookie, "application/x-www-form-urlencoded", htmxHeaders())
70	
71		// On HTMX request, the handler returns 200 with empty body.
72		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK && resp.StatusCode != http.StatusFound {
73			t.Fatalf("expected 200 or 302 on approve, got %d; body: %s",
74				resp.StatusCode, readBody(resp))
75		}
76	
77		// Verify DB.
78		var status string
79		if err := db.QueryRow(`SELECT status FROM comments WHERE id = ?`, comment.ID).Scan(&status); err != nil {
80			t.Fatalf("query comment: %v", err)
81		}
82		if status != "active" {
83			t.Errorf("expected comment status 'active' after approve, got %q", status)
84		}
85	}
86	
87	// TestRemoveComment verifies POST /moderation/:id/remove transitions a collapsed
88	// comment to removed status in the DB.
89	func TestRemoveComment(t *testing.T) {
90		app, db, cleanup := newTestApp(t)
91		defer cleanup()
92	
93		author := createTestUser(t, db, "student")
94		mat := createTestMaterial(t, db)
95	
96		engRepo := repository.NewEngagementRepository(db)

100		}

102			t.Fatalf("set collapsed: %v", err)
103		}
104	
105		modCookie := loginAs(t, app, db, "moderator")
106	
107		resp := makeRequest(app, http.MethodPost,
108			fmt.Sprintf("/moderation/%d/remove", comment.ID),
109			"", modCookie, "application/x-www-form-urlencoded", htmxHeaders())
110	
111		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK && resp.StatusCode != http.StatusFound {
112			t.Fatalf("expected 200 or 302 on remove, got %d; body: %s",
113				resp.StatusCode, readBody(resp))
114		}
115	
116		var status string
117		if err := db.QueryRow(`SELECT status FROM comments WHERE id = ?`, comment.ID).Scan(&status); err != nil {
118			t.Fatalf("query comment: %v", err)
119		}
120		if status != "removed" {
121			t.Errorf("expected comment status 'removed' after remove, got %q", status)
122		}
123	}

125	// TestApproveComment_WrongStatus verifies that approving an already-active
126	// comment returns 422 (the repository enforces status='collapsed').
127	func TestApproveComment_WrongStatus(t *testing.T) {
128		app, db, cleanup := newTestApp(t)

146	
147		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusUnprocessableEntity {
148			t.Fatalf("expected 422 on approve of non-collapsed comment, got %d", resp.StatusCode)
149		}

    14	// newOrderTestDB creates an in-memory SQLite database with the minimal schema
    15	// required for OrderService tests.
    16	func newOrderTestDB(t *testing.T) *sql.DB {
    17		t.Helper()
    18		// Use foreign_keys=off so that actor_id=0 in order_events does not cause
    19		// FK violations when the scheduler's auto-close passes a system actor ID of 0.
    20		db, err := sql.Open("sqlite3", ":memory:")
    21		if err != nil {
    22			t.Fatalf("newOrderTestDB: open: %v", err)
    23		}
    24		t.Cleanup(func() { db.Close() })
    25	
    26		schema := `
    27			CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    28				id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    29				username        TEXT    UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    30				email           TEXT    NOT NULL,
    31				password_hash   TEXT    NOT NULL,
    32				role            TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'student',
    33				failed_attempts INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    34				locked_until    TEXT,
    35				created_at      TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    36				updated_at      TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    37				deleted_at      TEXT
    38			);
    39			CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS materials (
    40				id            INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    41				isbn          TEXT,
    42				title         TEXT    NOT NULL,
    43				author        TEXT,
    44				publisher     TEXT,
    45				edition       TEXT,
    46				subject       TEXT,
    47				grade_level   TEXT,
    48				total_qty     INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    49				available_qty INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    50				reserved_qty  INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    51				status        TEXT    DEFAULT 'active',
    52				created_at    TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    53				updated_at    TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    54				deleted_at    TEXT
    55			);
    56			CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS orders (
    57				id            INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    58				user_id       INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    59				status        TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending_payment',
    60				total_amount  REAL    DEFAULT 0,
    61				auto_close_at TEXT,
    62				created_at    TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    63				updated_at    TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    64			);
    65			CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS order_items (
    66				id                 INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    67				order_id           INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES orders(id),
    68				material_id        INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES materials(id),
    69				qty                INTEGER NOT NULL,
    70				unit_price         REAL    DEFAULT 0,
    71				fulfillment_status TEXT    DEFAULT 'pending'
    72			);
    73			CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS order_events (
    74				id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    75				order_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES orders(id),
    76				from_status TEXT,
    77				to_status   TEXT    NOT NULL,
    78				actor_id    INTEGER REFERENCES users(id),
    79				note        TEXT,
    80				created_at  TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    81			);
    82			CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS return_requests (
    83				id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    84				order_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES orders(id),
    85				user_id     INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    86				type        TEXT    NOT NULL,
    87				status      TEXT    DEFAULT 'pending',
    88				reason      TEXT,
    89				requested_at TEXT   DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    90				resolved_at TEXT,
    91				resolved_by INTEGER REFERENCES users(id)
    92			);`
    93	
    94		if _, err := db.Exec(schema); err != nil {
    95			t.Fatalf("newOrderTestDB: schema: %v", err)
    96		}
    97		return db
    98	}
    99	
   100	// seedUser inserts a test user and returns its ID.
   101	func seedUser(t *testing.T, db *sql.DB) int64 {
   102		t.Helper()
   103		res, err := db.Exec(`
   104			INSERT INTO users (username, email, password_hash, role)
   105			VALUES ('testuser', 'test@example.com', 'hash', 'student')`)
   106		if err != nil {
   107			t.Fatalf("seedUser: %v", err)
   108		}
   109		id, _ := res.LastInsertId()
   110		return id
   111	}
   112	
   113	// seedMaterial inserts a material with the given available qty and status,
   114	// returns its ID.
   115	func seedMaterial(t *testing.T, db *sql.DB, title string, availQty int, status string) int64 {
   116		t.Helper()
   117		res, err := db.Exec(`
   118			INSERT INTO materials (title, total_qty, available_qty, reserved_qty, status)
   119			VALUES (?, ?, ?, 0, ?)`,
   120			title, availQty, availQty, status)
   121		if err != nil {
   122			t.Fatalf("seedMaterial %q: %v", title, err)
   123		}
   124		id, _ := res.LastInsertId()
   125		return id
   126	}
   127	
   128	// newOrderService builds an OrderService wired to the given test DB.
   129	func newOrderService(t *testing.T, db *sql.DB) *services.OrderService {
   130		t.Helper()
   131		orderRepo    := repository.NewOrderRepository(db)
   132		materialRepo := repository.NewMaterialRepository(db)
   133		return services.NewOrderService(orderRepo, materialRepo)
   134	}

   280	func TestAutoClose_CancelsOverdueOrders(t *testing.T) {
   281		db  := newOrderTestDB(t)
   282	
   283		userID := seedUser(t, db)
   284		matID  := seedMaterial(t, db, "Biology Book", 10, "active")
   285	
   286		// Insert an order that is already overdue for auto-close.
   287		// SQLite's datetime('now') uses "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS" format, so we must
   288		// store auto_close_at in the same format for the comparison to work.
   289		pastTime := time.Now().UTC().Add(-1 * time.Hour).Format("2006-01-02 15:04:05")
   290		_, err := db.Exec(`
   291			INSERT INTO orders (user_id, status, total_amount, auto_close_at)
   292			VALUES (?, 'pending_payment', 10.00, ?)`,
   293			userID, pastTime)
   294		if err != nil {
   295			t.Fatalf("insert overdue order: %v", err)
   296		}
   297		var orderID int64
   298		if err := db.QueryRow(`SELECT id FROM orders ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1`).Scan(&orderID); err != nil {
   299			t.Fatalf("get order id: %v", err)
   300		}
   301	
   302		// Insert an order_item for the overdue order so inventory can be rolled back.
   303		_, err = db.Exec(`
   304			INSERT INTO order_items (order_id, material_id, qty, unit_price, fulfillment_status)
   305			VALUES (?, ?, 2, 5.00, 'pending')`,
   306			orderID, matID)
   307		if err != nil {
   308			t.Fatalf("insert order_item: %v", err)
   309		}
   310	
   311		// Reserve 2 units so the rollback has something to undo.
   312		if _, err := db.Exec(`UPDATE materials SET available_qty = available_qty - 2, reserved_qty = reserved_qty + 2 WHERE id = ?`, matID); err != nil {
   313			t.Fatalf("reserve inventory: %v", err)
   314		}
   315	
   316		orderRepo    := repository.NewOrderRepository(db)
   317		materialRepo := repository.NewMaterialRepository(db)
   318	
   319		closed, err := orderRepo.CloseOverdueOrders(materialRepo)
   320		if err != nil {
   321			t.Fatalf("CloseOverdueOrders: %v", err)
   322		}
   323		if closed != 1 {
   324			t.Errorf("expected 1 order closed, got %d", closed)
   325		}
   326	
   327		// Verify status.
   328		var status string
   329		if err := db.QueryRow(`SELECT status FROM orders WHERE id = ?`, orderID).Scan(&status); err != nil {
   330			t.Fatalf("query status: %v", err)
   331		}
   332		if status != "canceled" {
   333			t.Errorf("expected status 'canceled', got %q", status)
   334		}
   335	
   336		// Verify inventory was restored.
   337		var avail int
   338		if err := db.QueryRow(`SELECT available_qty FROM materials WHERE id = ?`, matID).Scan(&avail); err != nil {
   339			t.Fatalf("query available_qty: %v", err)
   340		}
   341		if avail != 10 {
   342			t.Errorf("expected available_qty=10 after auto-close, got %d", avail)
   343		}
   344	}

    25	
    26		schema := `
    27			CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS users (
    28				id              INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    29				username        TEXT    UNIQUE NOT NULL,
    30				email           TEXT    NOT NULL,
    31				password_hash   TEXT    NOT NULL,
    32				role            TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'student',
    33				failed_attempts INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    34				locked_until    TEXT,

    37				deleted_at      TEXT
    38			);
    39			CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS materials (
    40				id            INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    41				isbn          TEXT,

    54				deleted_at    TEXT
    55			);
    56			CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS orders (
    57				id            INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,

    59				status        TEXT    NOT NULL DEFAULT 'pending_payment',
    60				total_amount  REAL    DEFAULT 0,

    69				qty                INTEGER NOT NULL,

    73			CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS order_events (

    78				actor_id    INTEGER REFERENCES users(id),
    79				note        TEXT,
    80				created_at  TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
    81			);
    82			CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS return_requests (
    83				id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    84				order_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES orders(id),
    85				user_id     INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    86				type        TEXT    NOT NULL,
    87				status      TEXT    DEFAULT 'pending',
    88				reason      TEXT,
    89				requested_at TEXT   DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    90				resolved_at TEXT,
    91				resolved_by INTEGER REFERENCES users(id)
    92			);`
    93	
    94		if _, err := db.Exec(schema); err != nil {

279	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
280	// Instructor: course order stats
281	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
282	
283	// CourseOrderStats returns per-subject/grade order aggregates for all orders
284	// whose items map to materials with the given instructor's course subjects.
285	// Since orders are user-scoped, this approximates course-level stats by
286	// joining materials on subject/grade_level filtered to orders placed by
287	// users in the instructor's reach (here: all orders, scoped by instructorID
288	// being the creator in a hypothetical course_plans table; for now we use
289	// the materials table directly and group by subject/grade).
290	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) CourseOrderStats(instructorID int64) ([]CourseOrderStat, error) {
291		const q = `
292			SELECT
293				COALESCE(m.subject, 'Unknown')     AS subject,
294				COALESCE(m.grade_level, 'Unknown') AS grade,
295				COUNT(oi.id)                        AS ordered,
296				SUM(CASE WHEN o.status IN ('pending_payment','pending_shipment') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS pending,
297				SUM(CASE WHEN oi.fulfillment_status = 'fulfilled' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)               AS fulfilled
298			FROM   order_items oi
299			JOIN   orders   o ON o.id = oi.order_id
300			JOIN   materials m ON m.id = oi.material_id
301			WHERE  m.deleted_at IS NULL
302			GROUP  BY m.subject, m.grade_level
303			ORDER  BY subject, grade`
304	
305		rows, err := r.db.Query(q)
306		if err != nil {
307			return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: CourseOrderStats: %w", err)
308		}
309		defer rows.Close()
310	
311		var out []CourseOrderStat
312		for rows.Next() {
313			var s CourseOrderStat
314			if err := rows.Scan(&s.Subject, &s.Grade, &s.Ordered, &s.Pending, &s.Fulfilled); err != nil {

283	// CourseOrderStats returns per-subject/grade order aggregates for all orders
284	// whose items map to materials with the given instructor's course subjects.
285	// Since orders are user-scoped, this approximates course-level stats by
286	// joining materials on subject/grade_level filtered to orders placed by
287	// users in the instructor's reach (here: all orders, scoped by instructorID
288	// being the creator in a hypothetical course_plans table; for now we use
289	// the materials table directly and group by subject/grade).
290	func (r *AnalyticsRepository) CourseOrderStats(instructorID int64) ([]CourseOrderStat, error) {
291		const q = `
292			SELECT
293				COALESCE(m.subject, 'Unknown')     AS subject,
294				COALESCE(m.grade_level, 'Unknown') AS grade,
295				COUNT(oi.id)                        AS ordered,
296				SUM(CASE WHEN o.status IN ('pending_payment','pending_shipment') THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS pending,
297				SUM(CASE WHEN oi.fulfillment_status = 'fulfilled' THEN 1 ELSE 0 END)               AS fulfilled
298			FROM   order_items oi
299			JOIN   orders   o ON o.id = oi.order_id
300			JOIN   materials m ON m.id = oi.material_id
301			WHERE  m.deleted_at IS NULL
302			GROUP  BY m.subject, m.grade_level
303			ORDER  BY subject, grade`
304	
305		rows, err := r.db.Query(q)
306		if err != nil {
307			return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: CourseOrderStats: %w", err)
308		}
309		defer rows.Close()
310	
311		var out []CourseOrderStat
312		for rows.Next() {
313			var s CourseOrderStat
314			if err := rows.Scan(&s.Subject, &s.Grade, &s.Ordered, &s.Pending, &s.Fulfilled); err != nil {
315				return nil, fmt.Errorf("analytics: CourseOrderStats scan: %w", err)
316			}
317			out = append(out, s)
318		}
319		return out, rows.Err()
320	}

10	// CourseRepository provides database operations for courses, course sections,
11	// and course material demand plans.
12	type CourseRepository struct {
13		db *sql.DB
14	}
15	
16	// NewCourseRepository returns a CourseRepository backed by the given database.
17	func NewCourseRepository(db *sql.DB) *CourseRepository {
18		return &CourseRepository{db: db}
19	}
20	
21	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
22	// Courses
23	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
24	
25	// CreateCourse inserts a new course row and returns the populated model.
26	func (r *CourseRepository) CreateCourse(instructorID int64, name, subject, gradeLevel, academicYear string) (*models.Course, error) {
27		const q = `
28			INSERT INTO courses (instructor_id, name, subject, grade_level, academic_year)
29			VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
30			RETURNING id, instructor_id, name, subject, grade_level, academic_year, created_at, updated_at`
31	
32		row := r.db.QueryRow(q, instructorID,
33			nullStr(name), nullStr(subject), nullStr(gradeLevel), nullStr(academicYear))
34		c := &models.Course{}
35		if err := row.Scan(&c.ID, &c.InstructorID, &c.Name, &c.Subject,
36			&c.GradeLevel, &c.AcademicYear, &c.CreatedAt, &c.UpdatedAt); err != nil {
37			return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: CreateCourse: %w", err)
38		}
39		return c, nil
40	}
41	
42	// GetCoursesByInstructor returns all courses owned by the given instructor.
43	func (r *CourseRepository) GetCoursesByInstructor(instructorID int64) ([]models.Course, error) {
44		const q = `
45			SELECT id, instructor_id, name, subject, grade_level, academic_year, created_at, updated_at
46			FROM   courses
47			WHERE  instructor_id = ?
48			ORDER  BY name`
49	
50		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, instructorID)
51		if err != nil {
52			return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: GetCoursesByInstructor: %w", err)
53		}
54		defer rows.Close()
55		return scanCourses(rows)
56	}
57	
58	// GetCourseByID returns a single course by its primary key.
59	func (r *CourseRepository) GetCourseByID(id int64) (*models.Course, error) {
60		const q = `
61			SELECT id, instructor_id, name, subject, grade_level, academic_year, created_at, updated_at
62			FROM   courses
63			WHERE  id = ?`
64	
65		row := r.db.QueryRow(q, id)
66		c := &models.Course{}
67		if err := row.Scan(&c.ID, &c.InstructorID, &c.Name, &c.Subject,
68			&c.GradeLevel, &c.AcademicYear, &c.CreatedAt, &c.UpdatedAt); err != nil {
69			if err == sql.ErrNoRows {
70				return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: GetCourseByID: course %d not found", id)
71			}

73		}
74		return c, nil

86			RETURNING id, course_id, name, period, room, created_at`

98		const q = `

100			FROM   course_sections
101			WHERE  course_id = ?
102			ORDER  BY name`
103	
104		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, courseID)
105		if err != nil {

107		}
108		defer rows.Close()
109	
110		var out []models.CourseSection
111		for rows.Next() {

114				return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: GetSectionsByCourse: scan: %w", err)
115			}

117		}
118		return out, rows.Err()

122	// Course Plans
123	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
124	
125	// UpsertPlanItem inserts or updates a material demand line for a course.
126	func (r *CourseRepository) UpsertPlanItem(courseID, materialID int64, requestedQty int, notes string) (*models.CoursePlan, error) {
127		const q = `
128			INSERT INTO course_plans (course_id, material_id, requested_qty, notes, status)
129			VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, 'pending')
130			ON CONFLICT(course_id, material_id) DO UPDATE SET
131				requested_qty = excluded.requested_qty,
132				notes         = excluded.notes,
133				updated_at    = datetime('now')
134			RETURNING id, course_id, material_id, requested_qty, approved_qty, status, notes, created_at, updated_at`
135	
136		row := r.db.QueryRow(q, courseID, materialID, requestedQty, nullStr(notes))
137		return scanCoursePlan(row)
138	}
139	
140	// ApprovePlanItem sets approved_qty and transitions status to 'approved'.
141	func (r *CourseRepository) ApprovePlanItem(planID int64, approvedQty int) error {
142		const q = `
143			UPDATE course_plans
144			SET    approved_qty = ?, status = 'approved', updated_at = datetime('now')
145			WHERE  id = ? AND status = 'pending'`
146		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, approvedQty, planID)
147		return err
148	}
149	
150	// GetPlansByCourse returns all demand plan items for a course.
151	func (r *CourseRepository) GetPlansByCourse(courseID int64) ([]models.CoursePlan, error) {
152		const q = `
153			SELECT id, course_id, material_id, requested_qty, approved_qty, status, notes, created_at, updated_at
154			FROM   course_plans
155			WHERE  course_id = ?
156			ORDER  BY created_at`
157	
158		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, courseID)
159		if err != nil {
160			return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: GetPlansByCourse: %w", err)
161		}
162		defer rows.Close()
163	
164		var out []models.CoursePlan
165		for rows.Next() {
166			var p models.CoursePlan
167			if err := rows.Scan(&p.ID, &p.CourseID, &p.MaterialID, &p.RequestedQty,
168				&p.ApprovedQty, &p.Status, &p.Notes, &p.CreatedAt, &p.UpdatedAt); err != nil {
169				return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: GetPlansByCourse: scan: %w", err)
170			}
171			out = append(out, p)
172		}
173		return out, rows.Err()
174	}

176	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
177	// helpers
178	// ---------------------------------------------------------------
179	
180	func nullStr(s string) interface{} {
181		if s == "" {
182			return nil
183		}
184		return s
185	}
186	
187	func scanCourses(rows *sql.Rows) ([]models.Course, error) {
188		var out []models.Course
189		for rows.Next() {
190			c := models.Course{}
191			if err := rows.Scan(&c.ID, &c.InstructorID, &c.Name, &c.Subject,
192				&c.GradeLevel, &c.AcademicYear, &c.CreatedAt, &c.UpdatedAt); err != nil {
193				return nil, fmt.Errorf("scanCourses: %w", err)
194			}
195			out = append(out, c)
196		}
197		return out, rows.Err()
198	}
199	
200	func scanCoursePlan(row *sql.Row) (*models.CoursePlan, error) {
201		p := &models.CoursePlan{}
202		if err := row.Scan(&p.ID, &p.CourseID, &p.MaterialID, &p.RequestedQty,
203			&p.ApprovedQty, &p.Status, &p.Notes, &p.CreatedAt, &p.UpdatedAt); err != nil {
204			return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: scanCoursePlan: %w", err)
205		}
206		return p, nil
207	}

144			SET    approved_qty = ?, status = 'approved', updated_at = datetime('now')
145			WHERE  id = ? AND status = 'pending'`
146		_, err := r.db.Exec(q, approvedQty, planID)
147		return err
148	}

150	// GetPlansByCourse returns all demand plan items for a course.
151	func (r *CourseRepository) GetPlansByCourse(courseID int64) ([]models.CoursePlan, error) {
152		const q = `
153			SELECT id, course_id, material_id, requested_qty, approved_qty, status, notes, created_at, updated_at
154			FROM   course_plans
155			WHERE  course_id = ?
156			ORDER  BY created_at`
157	
158		rows, err := r.db.Query(q, courseID)
159		if err != nil {
160			return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: GetPlansByCourse: %w", err)
161		}
162		defer rows.Close()
163	
164		var out []models.CoursePlan
165		for rows.Next() {
166			var p models.CoursePlan
167			if err := rows.Scan(&p.ID, &p.CourseID, &p.MaterialID, &p.RequestedQty,
168				&p.ApprovedQty, &p.Status, &p.Notes, &p.CreatedAt, &p.UpdatedAt); err != nil {
169				return nil, fmt.Errorf("repository: GetPlansByCourse: scan: %w", err)
170			}
171			out = append(out, p)
172		}
173		return out, rows.Err()
174	}

183		}

187	func scanCourses(rows *sql.Rows) ([]models.Course, error) {
188		var out []models.Course
189		for rows.Next() {
190			c := models.Course{}
191			if err := rows.Scan(&c.ID, &c.InstructorID, &c.Name, &c.Subject,
192				&c.GradeLevel, &c.AcademicYear, &c.CreatedAt, &c.UpdatedAt); err != nil {
193				return nil, fmt.Errorf("scanCourses: %w", err)
194			}
195			out = append(out, c)
196		}
197		return out, rows.Err()
198	}

{"replace_all": false, "file_path": "/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/migrations/001_schema.sql", "old_string": "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS course_plans (
    id             INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    course_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES courses(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    material_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES materials(id),
    requested_qty  INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    approved_qty   INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    status         TEXT    DEFAULT 'pending',
    notes          TEXT,
    created_at     TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    updated_at     TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    UNIQUE(course_id, material_id)
);", "new_string": "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS course_plans (
    id             INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    course_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES courses(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    section_id     INTEGER REFERENCES course_sections(id),
    material_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES materials(id),
    requested_qty  INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    approved_qty   INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    status         TEXT    DEFAULT 'pending',
    notes          TEXT,
    created_at     TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    updated_at     TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);
-- Unique plan per (course, section, material); treats NULL section as 0 so
-- two "whole-course" rows for the same material are still prevented.
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS uq_course_plans_section
    ON course_plans(course_id, COALESCE(section_id, 0), material_id);"}

{"file_path": "/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/migrations/007_course_plans_section.sql", "content": "-- Add section_id to course_plans for per-section demand granularity.
-- SQLite does not support DROP CONSTRAINT, so we recreate the table.

ALTER TABLE course_plans RENAME TO course_plans_old;

CREATE TABLE course_plans (
    id             INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    course_id      INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES courses(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    section_id     INTEGER REFERENCES course_sections(id),
    material_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES materials(id),
    requested_qty  INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    approved_qty   INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    status         TEXT    DEFAULT 'pending',
    notes          TEXT,
    created_at     TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    updated_at     TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

INSERT INTO course_plans
    (id, course_id, section_id, material_id, requested_qty, approved_qty,
     status, notes, created_at, updated_at)
SELECT id, course_id, NULL, material_id, requested_qty, approved_qty,
       status, notes, created_at, updated_at
FROM   course_plans_old;

DROP TABLE course_plans_old;

CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS uq_course_plans_section
    ON course_plans(course_id, COALESCE(section_id, 0), material_id);
"}

All tests pass. Here's a precise accounting of every change made:

---

**Issue 1 — Secrets (`.env`)**

Replaced the two real hex values in `.env` with obvious non-functional placeholders:
- `ENCRYPTION_KEY=<replace-with-32-byte-hex-encoded-key>`
- `SESSION_SECRET=<replace-with-long-random-secret>`

The config layer already reads these from env vars and `Validate()` already fails fast on startup if they are absent — so the only change needed was the `.env` file itself.

---

**Issue 2 — Course Planning section granularity + concrete analytics**

*Schema (`migrations/001_schema.sql` + new `007_course_plans_section.sql`)*
- Removed the inline `UNIQUE(course_id, material_id)` from `course_plans`
- Added `section_id INTEGER REFERENCES course_sections(id)` (nullable)
- Added `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX uq_course_plans_section ON course_plans(course_id, COALESCE(section_id, 0), material_id)` — COALESCE(null,0) ensures two "whole-course" rows for the same material are still blocked
- `007_course_plans_section.sql` recreates the table for existing databases

*Model (`models.go`)* — Added `SectionID *int64` and `SectionName *string` to `CoursePlan`

*Repository (`repository/courses.go`)* — `UpsertPlanItem` now accepts `sectionID *int64`; `GetPlansByCourse` LEFT JOINs `course_sections` to populate `SectionName`; `scanCoursePlan` scans the new column

*Service (`services/courses.go`)* — `AddPlanItem` threaded `sectionID *int64` through

*Handler (`handlers/courses.go`)* — Parses optional `section_id` form field

*Analytics repository (`repository/analytics.go`)* — Replaced the approximation comment and the `subject/grade` query with a concrete JOIN across `courses → course_plans → course_sections → materials → order_items`; new `CourseOrderStat` struct has `CourseName`, `SectionName`, `MaterialTitle`, `RequestedQty`, `ApprovedQty`, `PlanStatus`, `Ordered`, `Fulfilled`; added `CountPendingPlanItems` to count actual pending rows

*Analytics service (`services/analytics.go`)* — `InstructorDashboardData` now calls `CountPendingPlanItems` instead of summing a stale `Pending` field

*Templates* — `analytics/instructor_dashboard.html` updated to show course/section/material columns; `courses/detail.html` shows Section column in the plan table and a section select dropdown in the Add Plan form

---

**Issue 3 — Integration test strictness**

- `helpers_test.go:13-15` — replaced "we accept any 2xx / or 500 if template missing" language with a clear statement that 500 is never acceptable
- `messaging_test.go:57-60` (`TestInbox_ShowsNotification`) — changed weak "not 401" guard to `== http.StatusOK`, and added a body-content assertion that the notification title appears in the rendered HTML
- `messaging_test.go:132-134` (`TestMarkAllRead`) — changed weak "not 401/403" guard to `== http.StatusOK || == http.StatusFound`

    Verification: Ensure the solution is compatible with the project's database driver (SQLite/Postgres).

Execution:

content.arguments:
{"file_path": "/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/migrations/005_add_external_id.sql", "content": "-- 005_add_external_id.sql
-- Adds a dedicated external_id column to the users table.
-- external_id holds a student/employee/institution-issued identifier that is
-- stable across account re-creation and is used as the "exact identifier" signal
-- in duplicate-user detection (score = 1.0 when two accounts share the same
-- non-null external_id).
--
-- SQLite prohibits ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN with a UNIQUE constraint when the
-- table already contains rows ("Cannot add a UNIQUE column").  The two-step
-- approach below is functionally identical to a column-level UNIQUE declaration:
--   1. Add the column without any constraint (existing rows receive NULL).
--   2. Build a unique index.  SQLite allows multiple NULLs in a UNIQUE index
--      because NULL is not considered equal to NULL, which matches the
--      semantics of the inline UNIQUE keyword on the base schema column.

ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN external_id TEXT;

CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS uq_users_external_id ON users(external_id);
"}

All green. Here's exactly what was changed and why:

---

**Root cause**

SQLite enforces a rule: `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN <col> UNIQUE` is only valid when the table has zero rows. The moment there is any existing data, SQLite refuses with *"Cannot add a UNIQUE column"*. This is documented behaviour — the engine has no way to guarantee uniqueness for the NULL values that would be assigned to every existing row during the `ADD COLUMN` operation (even though SQLite actually treats multiple NULLs as non-conflicting, the parser rejects the combination early).

**The fix (`migrations/005_add_external_id.sql`)**

The single-line migration was split into two idempotent steps:

```sql
-- Step 1: add the column without any constraint.
-- All existing rows receive NULL, which is always safe.
ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN external_id TEXT;

-- Step 2: create the unique index explicitly.
-- SQLite's column-level UNIQUE keyword compiles down to exactly this.
-- IF NOT EXISTS makes this statement safe to re-run after a partial failure.
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS uq_users_external_id ON users(external_id);
```

This is semantically identical to the original intent:
- Non-NULL values must be globally unique — enforced by the index.
- Multiple rows may hold NULL — SQLite UNIQUE indexes treat NULL as unknown (not equal to another NULL), so this is permitted automatically.
- The `IF NOT EXISTS` guard ensures the index creation is idempotent if the migration runner retries after a crash between the two statements (the whole file runs inside a single transaction, so in practice this cannot happen — but the guard costs nothing).

**The test (`internal/db/migration005_test.go`)**

Directly executes the migration SQL against an in-memory SQLite database seeded with 3 pre-existing rows, then asserts:
1. The migration applies without error
2. A unique value is accepted
3. A duplicate value is rejected
4. Multiple NULLs are permitted

1	# ============================================================
2	# Stage 1 — builder
3	# ============================================================
4	FROM golang:1.22-bookworm AS builder
5	
6	# CGO is required by github.com/mattn/go-sqlite3
7	RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
8	    gcc \
9	    libc-dev \
10	    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
11	
12	ENV CGO_ENABLED=1
13	
14	WORKDIR /build
15	
16	# Download dependencies first (layer cache)
17	COPY go.mod go.sum ./
18	RUN go mod download
19	
20	# Copy source and build
21	COPY . .
22	RUN go build -tags sqlite_fts5 -o /app/server ./cmd/server
23	
24	# ============================================================
25	# Stage 2 — runtime
26	# ============================================================
27	FROM debian:bookworm-slim
28	
29	RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
30	    ca-certificates \
31	    libsqlite3-0 \
32	    wget \
33	    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*
34	
35	# Non-root user
36	RUN groupadd --gid 1001 appuser && \
37	    useradd --uid 1001 --gid 1001 --no-create-home --shell /usr/sbin/nologin appuser
38	
39	WORKDIR /app
40	
41	# Copy binary from builder
42	COPY --from=builder /app/server /app/server
43	
44	# Copy runtime assets
45	COPY web/ /app/web/
46	COPY migrations/ /app/migrations/
47	
48	# Persistent data directory owned by appuser
49	RUN mkdir -p /app/data && chown appuser:appuser /app/data
50	
51	VOLUME ["/app/data"]
52	
53	USER appuser
54	
55	EXPOSE 3000
56	
57	HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=10s --start-period=10s --retries=3 \
58	    CMD wget -qO- http://localhost:3000/health || exit 1
59	
60	ENTRYPOINT ["/app/server"]
61

Now I understand the complete failure chain:

1. **`portal_data` is a named Docker volume** — it persists across container rebuilds. The old database has migrations `001`–`006` tracked as applied (from when `001_schema.sql` did not yet contain the courses tables).
2. **On restart**, `007` is the only unapplied migration. It runs `ALTER TABLE course_plans RENAME TO course_plans_old` — but `course_plans` was never created in the old schema, so it fails immediately.
3. **Root cause**: the courses tables (`courses`, `course_sections`, `course_plans`) were added to `001_schema.sql` (which was already applied on the volume), but no migration exists to create them on databases that skipped that change.

The fix: rewrite `007` so it **first guarantees all prerequisite tables exist** (`CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS`) before executing the rename-and-rebuild dance. Every step must work in all three states a real database can be in.

{"file_path": "/Users/rollin/eaglepoint/w2t86/repo/migrations/007_course_plans_section.sql", "content": "-- 007_course_plans_section.sql
--
-- Goal: ensure the courses, course_sections, and course_plans tables exist
-- with the correct schema (course_plans.section_id, no old inline UNIQUE
-- constraint, unique index using COALESCE).
--
-- This migration must be idempotent across three possible database states:
--
--   State A — fresh install
--     001_schema.sql already created all three tables with the correct schema.
--     The CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS statements below are no-ops.
--     The rename dance rebuilds the table identically and re-creates the index
--     (the old index name is freed when course_plans_old is dropped).
--
--   State B — existing database that predates the courses feature
--     001_schema.sql was applied before courses tables were added to it.
--     course_plans does not exist at all.
--     The CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS for course_plans creates the table fresh
--     with section_id; the rename dance then rebuilds it and creates the index.
--
--   State C — existing database with courses tables but old course_plans schema
--     (UNIQUE(course_id, material_id) table constraint, no section_id column)
--     The CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS for course_plans is a no-op (table exists).
--     The rename dance replaces the old table with the new schema and drops the
--     old inline UNIQUE constraint, adding the COALESCE-based unique index.

-- -------------------------------------------------------
-- Step 1: Prerequisite tables
-- -------------------------------------------------------

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS courses (
    id            INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    instructor_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES users(id),
    name          TEXT    NOT NULL,
    subject       TEXT,
    grade_level   TEXT,
    academic_year TEXT,
    created_at    TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    updated_at    TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS course_sections (
    id         INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    course_id  INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES courses(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    name       TEXT    NOT NULL,
    period     TEXT,
    room       TEXT,
    created_at TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

-- Create course_plans if it does not yet exist (State B).
-- On State A/C this is a no-op; the rename dance below always runs to
-- normalise the schema and index in both remaining states.
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS course_plans (
    id            INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    course_id     INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES courses(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    section_id    INTEGER REFERENCES course_sections(id),
    material_id   INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES materials(id),
    requested_qty INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    approved_qty  INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    status        TEXT    DEFAULT 'pending',
    notes         TEXT,
    created_at    TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    updated_at    TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

-- -------------------------------------------------------
-- Step 2: Rebuild course_plans with the correct schema
--
-- By this point course_plans is guaranteed to exist (Step 1 ensures it).
-- The rename-recreate pattern:
--   * removes the old UNIQUE(course_id, material_id) table constraint (State C)
--   * is a schema-preserving no-op for States A and B (empty tables)
--   * preserves all existing plan rows
-- section_id is carried over as NULL because:
--   - State A/B: tables are empty (no rows to lose)
--   - State C: old schema had no section_id column; NULL is correct
-- -------------------------------------------------------

ALTER TABLE course_plans RENAME TO course_plans_old;

CREATE TABLE course_plans (
    id            INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    course_id     INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES courses(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    section_id    INTEGER REFERENCES course_sections(id),
    material_id   INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES materials(id),
    requested_qty INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    approved_qty  INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    status        TEXT    DEFAULT 'pending',
    notes         TEXT,
    created_at    TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    updated_at    TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

INSERT INTO course_plans
    (id, course_id, section_id, material_id, requested_qty, approved_qty,
     status, notes, created_at, updated_at)
SELECT id, course_id, NULL, material_id, requested_qty, approved_qty,
       status, notes, created_at, updated_at
FROM   course_plans_old;

DROP TABLE course_plans_old;

-- -------------------------------------------------------
-- Step 3: Unique index
--
-- By the time we reach here the old index name (if any) has been freed by
-- DROP TABLE course_plans_old, so IF NOT EXISTS will always create it.
-- COALESCE(section_id, 0) lets the index treat NULL section as a sentinel 0,
-- preventing duplicate whole-course plans for the same material.
-- -------------------------------------------------------

CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS uq_course_plans_section
    ON course_plans(course_id, COALESCE(section_id, 0), material_id);
"}

INSERT INTO course_plans

SELECT id, course_id, NULL, material_id, requested_qty, approved_qty,

    period     TEXT,
    room       TEXT,
    created_at TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

-- Create course_plans if it does not yet exist (State B).
-- On State A/C this is a no-op; the rename dance below always runs to
-- normalise the schema and index in both remaining states.
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS course_plans (
    id            INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    course_id     INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES courses(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    section_id    INTEGER REFERENCES course_sections(id),
    material_id   INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES materials(id),
    requested_qty INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    approved_qty  INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    status        TEXT    DEFAULT 'pending',
    notes         TEXT,
    created_at    TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    updated_at    TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

-- -------------------------------------------------------
-- Step 2: Rebuild course_plans with the correct schema
--
-- By this point course_plans is guaranteed to exist (Step 1 ensures it).
-- The rename-recreate pattern:
--   * removes the old UNIQUE(course_id, material_id) table constraint (State C)
--   * is a schema-preserving no-op for States A and B (empty tables)
--   * preserves all existing plan rows
-- section_id is carried over as NULL because:
--   - State A/B: tables are empty (no rows to lose)
--   - State C: old schema had no section_id column; NULL is correct
-- -------------------------------------------------------

ALTER TABLE course_plans RENAME TO course_plans_old;

CREATE TABLE course_plans (
    id            INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    course_id     INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES courses(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
    section_id    INTEGER REFERENCES course_sections(id),
    material_id   INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES materials(id),
    requested_qty INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    approved_qty  INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    status        TEXT    DEFAULT 'pending',
    notes         TEXT,
    created_at    TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    updated_at    TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

INSERT INTO course_plans
    (id, course_id, section_id, material_id, requested_qty, approved_qty,
     status, notes, created_at, updated_at)
SELECT id, course_id, NULL, material_id, requested_qty, approved_qty,
       status, notes, created_at, updated_at
FROM   course_plans_old;

DROP TABLE course_plans_old;

-- -------------------------------------------------------
-- Step 3: Unique index
--
-- By the time we reach here the old index name (if any) has been freed by
-- DROP TABLE course_plans_old, so IF NOT EXISTS will always create it.
-- COALESCE(section_id, 0) lets the index treat NULL section as a sentinel 0,
-- preventing duplicate whole-course plans for the same material.
-- -------------------------------------------------------

CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS uq_course_plans_section
    ON course_plans(course_id, COALESCE(section_id, 0), material_id);
"}

-- 003_financial_transactions.sql
-- Adds the financial_transactions table for return/refund/charge traceability.

CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS financial_transactions (
    id                INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    order_id          INTEGER REFERENCES orders(id),
    return_request_id INTEGER REFERENCES return_requests(id),
    type              TEXT    NOT NULL,  -- 'refund' | 'charge' | 'adjustment'
    amount            REAL    NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    status            TEXT    DEFAULT 'pending',  -- 'pending' | 'completed' | 'failed'
    reference         TEXT,
    note              TEXT,
    actor_id          INTEGER REFERENCES users(id),
    created_at        TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    updated_at        TEXT    DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
);

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_financial_txn_order  ON financial_transactions(order_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_financial_txn_return ON financial_transactions(return_request_id);

=== 004_add_full_name.sql ===
-- 004_add_full_name.sql
-- Adds the full_name column to the users table.
-- This provides a real-name field used for duplicate-user detection
-- (fuzzy name matching via Levenshtein distance + date_of_birth).
ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN full_name TEXT;

=== 005_add_external_id.sql ===
-- 005_add_external_id.sql
-- Adds a dedicated external_id column to the users table.
-- external_id holds a student/employee/institution-issued identifier that is
-- stable across account re-creation and is used as the "exact identifier" signal
-- in duplicate-user detection (score = 1.0 when two accounts share the same
-- non-null external_id).
--
-- SQLite prohibits ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN with a UNIQUE constraint when the
-- table already contains rows ("Cannot add a UNIQUE column").  The two-step
-- approach below is functionally identical to a column-level UNIQUE declaration:
--   1. Add the column without any constraint (existing rows receive NULL).
--   2. Build a unique index.  SQLite allows multiple NULLs in a UNIQUE index
--      because NULL is not considered equal to NULL, which matches the
--      semantics of the inline UNIQUE keyword on the base schema column.

ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN external_id TEXT;

CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS uq_users_external_id ON users(external_id);

-- 006_return_request_replacement.sql
-- Adds replacement_material_id to return_requests so exchange approvals can
-- validate that the replacement item has sufficient inventory.
ALTER TABLE return_requests
    ADD COLUMN replacement_material_id INTEGER REFERENCES materials(id);

2. Key Technical Concepts:
- **SQLite migration constraints**: `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN <col> UNIQUE` fails on non-empty tables; `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` does not exist; `ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE` without target fires on any unique violation
- **SQLite UNIQUE index with NULLs**: NULLs are not equal to each other; `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX ON tbl(col)` allows multiple NULLs; `COALESCE(section_id, 0)` in expression indexes treats NULL as sentinel 0
- **Migration idempotency pattern**: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` + rename-copy-drop dance
- **Named Docker volumes** (`portal_data:/app/data`) persist across container rebuilds — old DB has old schema with 001-006 already applied; new migrations must handle both fresh and upgrade paths
- **001_schema.sql modification problem**: In previous sessions, columns (`date_of_birth`, `full_name`, `external_id`) and tables (`courses`, `course_sections`, `course_plans`) were added to `001_schema.sql` directly. This causes "duplicate column" failures on fresh databases where `001` runs first, then incremental migrations 002/004/005/006 also try to add those same items
- **Go Fiber v2**, SQLite with `github.com/mattn/go-sqlite3`, CGO-enabled Docker build, multi-stage Dockerfile (builder + debian-slim runtime)
- **Migration runner** (`internal/db/db.go`): reads `./migrations/*.sql` sorted lexicographically, tracks applied files in `_migrations` table, runs each in a transaction

---

3. Files and Code Sections:

- **`migrations/007_course_plans_section.sql`** — Core fix. Rewrote from a bare rename dance to a three-state-aware migration:
  ```sql
  -- Step 1: Prerequisite tables (IF NOT EXISTS — no-op on States A/fresh)
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS courses (...);
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS course_sections (...);
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS course_plans (
      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
      course_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES courses(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      section_id INTEGER REFERENCES course_sections(id),   -- new column
      material_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES materials(id),
      requested_qty INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
      approved_qty INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
      status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending',
      notes TEXT,
      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
      updated_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
  );
  -- Step 2: Rename-recreate dance (always runs; handles all three states)
  ALTER TABLE course_plans RENAME TO course_plans_old;
  CREATE TABLE course_plans (...same schema with section_id...);
  INSERT INTO course_plans (id, course_id, section_id, material_id, ...)
  SELECT id, course_id, NULL, material_id, ...
  FROM course_plans_old;
  DROP TABLE course_plans_old;
  -- Step 3: Index (freed after DROP TABLE course_plans_old)
  CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS uq_course_plans_section
      ON course_plans(course_id, COALESCE(section_id, 0), material_id);
  ```
  Three states handled:
  - **State A (fresh)**: 001_schema.sql already created tables; IF NOT EXISTS are no-ops; rename dance rebuilds identically; index name freed by DROP and recreated

2. Key Technical Concepts:
- **SQLite migration constraints**: `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN <col> UNIQUE` fails on non-empty tables; `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS` does not exist; `ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE` without target fires on any unique violation
- **SQLite UNIQUE index with NULLs**: NULLs are not equal to each other; `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX ON tbl(col)` allows multiple NULLs; `COALESCE(section_id, 0)` in expression indexes treats NULL as sentinel 0
- **Migration idempotency pattern**: `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` + rename-copy-drop dance
- **Named Docker volumes** (`portal_data:/app/data`) persist across container rebuilds — old DB has old schema with 001-006 already applied; new migrations must handle both fresh and upgrade paths
- **001_schema.sql modification problem**: In previous sessions, columns (`date_of_birth`, `full_name`, `external_id`) and tables (`courses`, `course_sections`, `course_plans`) were added to `001_schema.sql` directly. This causes "duplicate column" failures on fresh databases where `001` runs first, then incremental migrations 002/004/005/006 also try to add those same items
- **Go Fiber v2**, SQLite with `github.com/mattn/go-sqlite3`, CGO-enabled Docker build, multi-stage Dockerfile (builder + debian-slim runtime)
- **Migration runner** (`internal/db/db.go`): reads `./migrations/*.sql` sorted lexicographically, tracks applied files in `_migrations` table, runs each in a transaction

---

3. Files and Code Sections:

- **`migrations/007_course_plans_section.sql`** — Core fix. Rewrote from a bare rename dance to a three-state-aware migration:
  ```sql
  -- Step 1: Prerequisite tables (IF NOT EXISTS — no-op on States A/fresh)
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS courses (...);
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS course_sections (...);
  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS course_plans (
      id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
      course_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES courses(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
      section_id INTEGER REFERENCES course_sections(id),   -- new column
      material_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES materials(id),
      requested_qty INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
      approved_qty INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
      status TEXT DEFAULT 'pending',
      notes TEXT,
      created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
      updated_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
  );
  -- Step 2: Rename-recreate dance (always runs; handles all three states)
  ALTER TABLE course_plans RENAME TO course_plans_old;
  CREATE TABLE course_plans (...same schema with section_id...);
  INSERT INTO course_plans (id, course_id, section_id, material_id, ...)
  SELECT id, course_id, NULL, material_id, ...
  FROM course_plans_old;
  DROP TABLE course_plans_old;
  -- Step 3: Index (freed after DROP TABLE course_plans_old)
  CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS uq_course_plans_section
      ON course_plans(course_id, COALESCE(section_id, 0), material_id);
  ```
  Three states handled:
  - **State A (fresh)**: 001_schema.sql already created tables; IF NOT EXISTS are no-ops; rename dance rebuilds identically; index name freed by DROP and recreated
  - **State B (old DB, no courses)**: IF NOT EXISTS creates tables fresh; rename dance rebuilds; index created
  - **State C (old DB with old course_plans schema)**: IF NOT EXISTS is no-op; rename dance drops old UNIQUE(course_id,material_id) constraint and adds section_id

- **`migrations/005_add_external_id.sql`** — Fixed the "Cannot add a UNIQUE column" error:
  ```sql
  -- Step 1: add column without constraint
  ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN external_id TEXT;
  -- Step 2: create unique index (equivalent to column-level UNIQUE; allows multiple NULLs)
  CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS uq_users_external_id ON users(external_id);
  ```

The root cause is clear: `001_schema.sql` was patched in previous sessions to include columns that migrations 002, 004, 005, and 006 also add. On a fresh install, 001 creates all columns, then those migrations fail with "duplicate column name." The fix is to revert 001 to its original form so each incremental migration does its own job.

350	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
351	// loginAs — creates a user with the given role, logs in, returns cookie string.
352	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
353	
354	// loginAs creates a user with the given role, sends POST /login, and returns the
355	// "session_token=<value>" cookie string suitable for use in subsequent requests.
356	// The returned string can be passed directly as the Cookie header value.
357	func loginAs(t *testing.T, app *fiber.App, db *sql.DB, role string) string {
358		t.Helper()
359	
360		// Create a unique username per call so parallel tests don't collide.
361		username := fmt.Sprintf("testuser_%s_%d", role, time.Now().UnixNano())
362		password := "TestPassword123!"
363		email := username + "@example.com"
364	
365		userRepo := repository.NewUserRepository(db)
366		sessionRepo := repository.NewSessionRepository(db)
367		cfg := &config.Config{SessionSecret: "test-secret"}
368		authSvc := services.NewAuthService(userRepo, sessionRepo, cfg)
369	
370		if _, err := authSvc.Register(username, email, password, role); err != nil {
371			t.Fatalf("loginAs: register user: %v", err)
372		}
373	
374		body := fmt.Sprintf("username=%s&password=%s", username, password)
375		req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodPost, "/login", strings.NewReader(body))
376		req.Header.Set("Content-Type", "application/x-www-form-urlencoded")
377	
378		resp, err := app.Test(req, -1)
379		if err != nil {
380			t.Fatalf("loginAs: app.Test: %v", err)
381		}
382		defer resp.Body.Close()
383	
384		// Login success redirects to /dashboard (302).
385		if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusFound && resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
386			b, _ := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
387			t.Fatalf("loginAs: unexpected status %d; body: %s", resp.StatusCode, string(b))
388		}
389	
390		// Extract the Set-Cookie header.
391		for _, c := range resp.Cookies() {
392			if c.Name == "session_token" {
393				return "session_token=" + c.Value
394			}
395		}
396		t.Fatal("loginAs: no session_token cookie found in response")
397		return ""
398	}
399	
400	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
401	// makeRequest — fires a request against the test Fiber app.
402	// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
403	
404	// makeRequest builds a request and calls app.Test.
405	// - method: HTTP verb ("GET", "POST", etc.)
406	// - path: request path ("/orders/1")
407	// - body: raw request body (empty string for no body)
408	// - cookie: cookie header value (e.g. "session_token=abc123"); empty for none
409	// - contentType: e.g. "application/x-www-form-urlencoded"; empty defaults to no Content-Type
410	func makeRequest(app *fiber.App, method, path, body, cookie, contentType string, extraHeaders ...map[string]string) *http.Response {
411		var bodyReader io.Reader
412		if body != "" {
413			bodyReader = strings.NewReader(body)
414		}
415		req := httptest.NewRequest(method, path, bodyReader)
416		if cookie != "" {
417			req.Header.Set("Cookie", cookie)
418		}
419		if contentType != "" {
420			req.Header.Set("Content-Type", contentType)
421		}
422		for _, h := range extraHeaders {
423			for k, v := range h {
424				req.Header.Set(k, v)
425			}
426		}
427		resp, err := app.Test(req, -1)
428		if err != nil {
429			// Return a synthetic 500 response on internal test errors.
430			return &http.Response{StatusCode: http.StatusInternalServerError}
431		}
432		return resp
433	}
434	
435	// htmxHeaders returns a map with HX-Request: true for use with makeRequest's
436	// extraHeaders parameter.
437	func htmxHeaders() map[string]string {
438		return map[string]string{"HX-Request": "true"}
439	}

1	package testutil

12	// NewTestDB creates an in-memory SQLite DB and runs all migrations.
13	// Uses ":memory:" DSN with foreign keys enabled. Call t.Cleanup to close.
14	func NewTestDB(t *testing.T) *sql.DB {
15		t.Helper()
16		return newTestDB(t, ":memory:?_foreign_keys=on&_journal_mode=WAL")
17	}
18	
19	// NewTestDBNoFK creates an in-memory SQLite DB with foreign key enforcement
20	// disabled.  Use this for tests that exercise code paths that deliberately
21	// pass system-level actor IDs (e.g. 0) that have no corresponding user row.
22	func NewTestDBNoFK(t *testing.T) *sql.DB {
23		t.Helper()
24		return newTestDB(t, ":memory:?_journal_mode=WAL")
25	}
26	
27	func newTestDB(t *testing.T, dsn string) *sql.DB {
28		t.Helper()
29		db, err := sql.Open("sqlite3", dsn)
30		if err != nil {
31			t.Fatalf("open test db: %v", err)
32		}
33		db.SetMaxOpenConns(1)
34		runMigrations(t, db)
35		t.Cleanup(func() { db.Close() })
36		return db
37	}
38	
39	// reStripFTS5 matches the FTS5 virtual table declaration, its associated
40	// triggers, and any single-line comment immediately before them so they can be
41	// removed when FTS5 is not compiled in.
42	var reStripFTS5 = regexp.MustCompile(
43		`(?is)` +
44			// Virtual table declaration
45			`CREATE\s+VIRTUAL\s+TABLE\s+IF\s+NOT\s+EXISTS\s+materials_fts\b.*?;` +
46			`|` +
47			// Single-line comments that describe the FTS block
48			`--[^\n]*fts[^\n]*\n` +
49			`|` +
50			// Trigger blocks (BEGIN … END;)
51			`CREATE\s+TRIGGER\s+IF\s+NOT\s+EXISTS\s+materials_fts_\w+\b.*?END\s*;`,
52	)
53	
54	// runMigrations reads migrations/001_schema.sql and executes it.
55	// If the SQLite driver does not support FTS5, the virtual table and its
56	// triggers are stripped from the migration SQL so the rest of the schema
57	// can still be created.
58	func runMigrations(t *testing.T, db *sql.DB) {
59		t.Helper()
60		path := findMigrationsPath(t)
61		data, err := os.ReadFile(path)
62		if err != nil {
63			t.Fatalf("read migration: %v", err)
64		}
65	
66		sql := string(data)
67	
68		// Probe for FTS5 support.
69		if _, probeErr := db.Exec("CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE IF NOT EXISTS _fts5_probe USING fts5(x)"); probeErr != nil {
70			// FTS5 not available — strip the virtual table + triggers.
71			sql = reStripFTS5.ReplaceAllString(sql, "")
72		} else {
73			// Clean up the probe table.
74			db.Exec("DROP TABLE IF EXISTS _fts5_probe") //nolint:errcheck
75		}
76	
77		if _, err := db.Exec(sql); err != nil {
78			t.Fatalf("run migration: %v", err)
79		}
80	}
81	
82	func findMigrationsPath(t *testing.T) string {
83		t.Helper()
84		if p := os.Getenv("MIGRATION_PATH"); p != "" {
85			return p
86		}
87		candidates := []string{
88			"../migrations/001_schema.sql",       // from API_tests/ (repo_root/API_tests)
89			"../../migrations/001_schema.sql",    // from internal/* packages
90			"../../../migrations/001_schema.sql", // from internal/sub/packages
91			"migrations/001_schema.sql",          // from repo root
92		}
93		for _, c := range candidates {
94			if _, err := os.Stat(c); err == nil {
95				return c
96			}
97		}
98		t.Fatal("cannot find migrations/001_schema.sql - set MIGRATION_PATH env var")
99		return ""
100	}

The container starts cleanly on a fresh database — all 7 migrations run successfully with no errors. Here's what was fixed:

**Root cause**: Previous sessions had patched `001_schema.sql` to include columns (`date_of_birth`, `full_name`, `external_id`, `replacement_material_id`) and tables (`courses`, `course_sections`, `course_plans`) that incremental migrations 002, 004, 005, 006, and 007 also tried to add. On a fresh install every migration ran in sequence, so 001 created everything, then 002–006 hit "duplicate column name."

**Changes made**:

1. **`migrations/001_schema.sql`** — Reverted to the original base schema: removed `date_of_birth`, `full_name`, `external_id` from users; removed `replacement_material_id` from return_requests; removed the `courses`/`course_sections`/`course_plans` tables and `uq_course_plans_section` index; removed `full_name` from the admin seed INSERT. Each column/table is now owned by exactly one migration.

2. **`internal/testutil/db.go`** — Updated from running only `001_schema.sql` to running all `*.sql` files in the migrations directory in lexicographic order, matching production behavior. The FTS5 probe runs once before the loop. The `MIGRATION_PATH` env var still works (accepts either a directory path or a legacy path to `001_schema.sql`).

1	# District Materials Commerce & Logistics Portal
2	
3	A web-based portal for managing district-wide distribution of educational materials. It provides role-aware workflows for students (browsing, ordering, favorites), instructors (course plans, approvals), clerks (distribution, ledger), moderators (comment queue), and administrators (users, analytics, settings). Built with Go, Fiber, SQLite, HTMX, and Alpine.js.
4	
5	## Prerequisites
6	
7	- Go 1.22 or later
8	- GCC (required to compile the `mattn/go-sqlite3` CGo driver)
9	  - macOS: `xcode-select --install`
10	  - Ubuntu/Debian: `sudo apt install gcc`
11	  - Windows: install [TDM-GCC](https://jmeubank.github.io/tdm-gcc/) or use WSL
12	
13	## Setup
14	
15	1. **Clone the repository**
16	
17	   ```bash
18	   git clone <repo-url>
19	   cd w2t86
20	   ```
21	
22	2. **Install Go dependencies**
23	
24	   ```bash
25	   go mod tidy
26	   ```
27	
28	3. **Download HTMX and Alpine.js**
29	
30	   Place the minified files in `web/static/js/`:
31	
32	   ```bash
33	   # HTMX 2.x
34	   curl -Lo web/static/js/htmx.min.js \
35	     https://unpkg.com/htmx.org@2/dist/htmx.min.js
36	
37	   # Alpine.js 3.x
38	   curl -Lo web/static/js/alpine.min.js \
39	     https://unpkg.com/alpinejs@3/dist/cdn.min.js
40	   ```
41	
42	4. **Configure environment**
43	
44	   ```bash
45	   cp .env.example .env
46	   # Edit .env and fill in ENCRYPTION_KEY and SESSION_SECRET
47	   ```
48	
49	   Generate a random encryption key (32 bytes = 64 hex chars):
50	
51	   ```bash
52	   openssl rand -hex 32
53	   ```
54	
55	5. **Run the server**
56	
57	   ```bash
58	   go run ./cmd/server
59	   ```
60	
61	   The server listens on `http://localhost:3000` by default.
62	
63	## Docker
64	
65	### Quick start
66	```bash
67	cp .env.example .env
68	# Edit .env: set ENCRYPTION_KEY and SESSION_SECRET
69	docker compose up -d
70	```
71	
72	### Development (with live template reload)
73	```bash
74	docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.dev.yml up
75	```
76	
77	### Useful commands
78	```bash
79	make docker-logs     # tail logs
80	make docker-down     # stop
81	make test            # run tests locally
82	```
83	
84	## Environment Variables
85	
86	```
87	PORT=3000
88	DB_PATH=data/portal.db
89	ENCRYPTION_KEY=replace_with_64_hex_chars_32_bytes
90	SESSION_SECRET=replace_with_random_secret
91	APP_ENV=development
92	```
93	
94	| Variable         | Description                                          | Default           |
95	|------------------|------------------------------------------------------|-------------------|
96	| `PORT`           | HTTP listen port                                     | `3000`            |
97	| `DB_PATH`        | Path to the SQLite database file                     | `data/portal.db`  |
98	| `ENCRYPTION_KEY` | 64-char hex string (32 bytes) for AES-256-GCM fields | *(required)*      |
99	| `SESSION_SECRET` | Random secret used to sign session tokens            | *(required)*      |
100	| `APP_ENV`        | `development` (hot-reload templates) or `production` | `development`     |
101	
102	## Default Credentials
103	
104	| Username | Password       | Role  |
105	|----------|----------------|-------|
106	| `admin`  | `ChangeMe123!` | admin |
107	
108	**Change the admin password immediately after first login.**
109	
110	The default admin account is inserted by the initial migration. Update it via the Admin Settings page or directly in the database.
111	
112	## Available Roles
113	
114	| Role         | Capabilities                                                        |
115	|--------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
116	| `student`    | Browse materials, place orders, manage favorites, inbox             |
117	| `instructor` | Course plans, approve orders, inbox                                 |
118	| `clerk`      | Distribution events, ledger, backorder management, inbox            |
119	| `moderator`  | Review and act on reported comments, inbox                          |
120	| `admin`      | Full access: user management, analytics, all settings, all of above |

1	package config
2	
3	import (
4		"errors"
5		"os"
6	)
7	
8	// Config holds all runtime configuration for the portal.
9	type Config struct {
10		Port          string // HTTP listen port, default "3000"
11		DBPath        string // SQLite file path, default "data/portal.db"
12		EncryptionKey string // 32-byte hex key for AES-256-GCM field encryption
13		SessionSecret string // secret used to sign/verify session tokens
14		AppEnv        string // "development" | "production"
15		BannedWords   string // comma-separated list of prohibited comment words
16	}
17	
18	// Load reads configuration from environment variables, applying defaults where
19	// a variable is absent or empty.
20	func Load() *Config {
21		return &Config{
22			Port:          envOrDefault("PORT", "3000"),
23			DBPath:        envOrDefault("DB_PATH", "data/portal.db"),
24			EncryptionKey: envOrDefault("ENCRYPTION_KEY", ""),
25			SessionSecret: envOrDefault("SESSION_SECRET", ""),
26			AppEnv:        envOrDefault("APP_ENV", "development"),
27			BannedWords:   envOrDefault("BANNED_WORDS", ""),
28		}
29	}
30	
31	// Validate returns an error if any required secret configuration is absent.
32	// Call this at startup so the application fails fast rather than running with
33	// an empty encryption key or session secret.
34	func (c *Config) Validate() error {
35		var errs []error
36		if c.EncryptionKey == "" {
37			errs = append(errs, errors.New("ENCRYPTION_KEY must be set"))
38		}
39		if c.SessionSecret == "" {
40			errs = append(errs, errors.New("SESSION_SECRET must be set"))
41		}
42		return errors.Join(errs...)
43	}
44	
45	// envOrDefault returns the value of the named environment variable, or
46	// fallback when the variable is unset or empty.
47	func envOrDefault(key, fallback string) string {
48		if v := os.Getenv(key); v != "" {
49			return v
50		}
51		return fallback
52	}
53

The application reads all configuration from environment variables.
There is **no committed `.env` file** — create one locally (it is gitignored) or
inject variables through your deployment platform.

### Minimal `.env` for local development

```dotenv
# --- Required secrets (generate with: openssl rand -hex 32) ---
ENCRYPTION_KEY=<64-hex-char string, 32 bytes>
SESSION_SECRET=<long random string>

# --- Optional: shown here with their default values ---
PORT=3000
DB_PATH=data/portal.db
APP_ENV=development
BANNED_WORDS=
```

### Variable reference

| Variable | Required | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `ENCRYPTION_KEY` | **yes** | — | 64-character hex string (32 bytes) used as the AES-256-GCM key for encrypting sensitive user custom fields. Generate with `openssl rand -hex 32`. |
| `SESSION_SECRET` | **yes** | — | Arbitrary secret string used to sign and verify session tokens. Use a long random value; `openssl rand -hex 32` is sufficient. |
| `PORT` | no | `3000` | TCP port the HTTP server binds to. |
| `DB_PATH` | no | `data/portal.db` | Filesystem path to the SQLite database file. The parent directory is created automatically on first run. In Docker the volume is mounted at `/app/data`, so use `/app/data/portal.db`. |
| `APP_ENV` | no | `development` | Runtime environment. Set to `production` to disable template hot-reload and enable stricter security defaults. Any other value is treated as development. |
| `BANNED_WORDS` | no | *(empty)* | Comma-separated list of words blocked in material comments (e.g. `spam,abuse`). Leave empty to disable the filter entirely. |"}

1	# District Materials Commerce & Logistics Portal
2	
3	A web-based portal for managing district-wide distribution of educational materials. It provides role-aware workflows for students (browsing, ordering, favorites), instructors (course plans, approvals), clerks (distribution, ledger), moderators (comment queue), and administrators (users, analytics, settings). Built with Go, Fiber, SQLite, HTMX, and Alpine.js.
4	
5	## Prerequisites
6	
7	- Go 1.22 or later
8	- GCC (required to compile the `mattn/go-sqlite3` CGo driver)
9	  - macOS: `xcode-select --install`
10	  - Ubuntu/Debian: `sudo apt install gcc`
11	  - Windows: install [TDM-GCC](https://jmeubank.github.io/tdm-gcc/) or use WSL
12	
13	## Setup
14	
15	1. **Clone the repository**
16	
17	   ```bash
18	   git clone <repo-url>
19	   cd w2t86
20	   ```
21	
22	2. **Install Go dependencies**
23	
24	   ```bash
25	   go mod tidy
26	   ```
27	
28	3. **Download HTMX and Alpine.js**
29	
30	   Place the minified files in `web/static/js/`:
31	
32	   ```bash
33	   # HTMX 2.x
34	   curl -Lo web/static/js/htmx.min.js \
35	     https://unpkg.com/htmx.org@2/dist/htmx.min.js
36	
37	   # Alpine.js 3.x
38	   curl -Lo web/static/js/alpine.min.js \
39	     https://unpkg.com/alpinejs@3/dist/cdn.min.js
40	   ```
41	
42	4. **Configure environment**
43	
44	   Create a `.env` file in the project root (it is gitignored — never commit it):
45	
46	   ```bash
47	   # Generate the two required secrets
48	   echo "ENCRYPTION_KEY=$(openssl rand -hex 32)" >> .env
49	   echo "SESSION_SECRET=$(openssl rand -hex 32)" >> .env
50	
51	   # Add the remaining variables with your preferred values
52	   echo "PORT=3000"            >> .env
53	   echo "DB_PATH=data/portal.db" >> .env
54	   echo "APP_ENV=development"  >> .env
55	   echo "BANNED_WORDS="        >> .env
56	   ```
57	
58	   See the [Environment Variables](#environment-variables) section below for a
59	   description of every variable.
60	
61	5. **Run the server**
62	
63	   ```bash
64	   go run ./cmd/server
65	   ```
66	
67	   The server listens on `http://localhost:3000` by default.
68	
69	## Docker
70	
71	### Quick start
72	```bash
73	# Create .env with generated secrets (gitignored — never commit this file)
74	echo "ENCRYPTION_KEY=$(openssl rand -hex 32)" >  .env
75	echo "SESSION_SECRET=$(openssl rand -hex 32)" >> .env
76	echo "PORT=3000"             >> .env
77	echo "DB_PATH=/app/data/portal.db" >> .env
78	echo "APP_ENV=production"    >> .env
79	
80	docker compose up -d
81	```
82	
83	### Development (with live template reload)
84	```bash
85	docker compose -f docker-compose.yml -f docker-compose.dev.yml up
86	```
87	
88	### Useful commands
89	```bash
90	make docker-logs     # tail logs
91	make docker-down     # stop
92	make test            # run tests locally
93	```
94	
95	## Environment Variables
96	
97	The application reads all configuration from environment variables.
98	There is **no committed `.env` file** — create one locally (it is gitignored) or
99	inject variables through your deployment platform.
100	
101	### Minimal `.env` for local development
102	
103	```dotenv
104	# --- Required secrets (generate with: openssl rand -hex 32) ---
105	ENCRYPTION_KEY=<64-hex-char string, 32 bytes>
106	SESSION_SECRET=<long random string>
107	
108	# --- Optional: shown here with their default values ---
109	PORT=3000
110	DB_PATH=data/portal.db
111	APP_ENV=development
112	BANNED_WORDS=
113	```
114	
115	### Variable reference
116	
117	| Variable | Required | Default | Description |
118	|---|---|---|---|
119	| `ENCRYPTION_KEY` | **yes** | — | 64-character hex string (32 bytes) used as the AES-256-GCM key for encrypting sensitive user custom fields. Generate with `openssl rand -hex 32`. |
120	| `SESSION_SECRET` | **yes** | — | Arbitrary secret string used to sign and verify session tokens. Use a long random value; `openssl rand -hex 32` is sufficient. |
121	| `PORT` | no | `3000` | TCP port the HTTP server binds to. |
122	| `DB_PATH` | no | `data/portal.db` | Filesystem path to the SQLite database file. The parent directory is created automatically on first run. In Docker the volume is mounted at `/app/data`, so use `/app/data/portal.db`. |
123	| `APP_ENV` | no | `development` | Runtime environment. Set to `production` to disable template hot-reload and enable stricter security defaults. Any other value is treated as development. |
124	| `BANNED_WORDS` | no | *(empty)* | Comma-separated list of words blocked in material comments (e.g. `spam,abuse`). Leave empty to disable the filter entirely. |
125	
126	## Default Credentials
127	
128	| Username | Password       | Role  |
129	|----------|----------------|-------|
130	| `admin`  | `ChangeMe123!` | admin |
131	
132	**Change the admin password immediately after first login.**
133	
134	The default admin account is inserted by the initial migration. Update it via the Admin Settings page or directly in the database.
135	
136	## Available Roles
137	
138	| Role         | Capabilities                                                        |
139	|--------------|---------------------------------------------------------------------|
140	| `student`    | Browse materials, place orders, manage favorites, inbox             |
141	| `instructor` | Course plans, approve orders, inbox                                 |
142	| `clerk`      | Distribution events, ledger, backorder management, inbox            |
143	| `moderator`  | Review and act on reported comments, inbox                          |
144	| `admin`      | Full access: user management, analytics, all settings, all of above |
145	
146	## Project Structure
147	
148	```
149	w2t86/
150	├── cmd/
151	│   └── server/
152	│       └── main.go              # Entry point: wires and starts the server
153	├── internal/

156	│   ├── crypto/

160	│   ├── handlers/

164	│   │   ├── ratelimit.go         # Sliding-window rate limiter
165	│   │   └── rbac.go              # Role-based access control
166	│   ├── models/

168	│   ├── repository/

173	│   └── services/

175	├── migrations/

183	│   │       ├── alpine.min.js    # Alpine.js 3.x (download separately)
